NEW ISSUE (BOOK-ENTRY-ONLY) RATING:  See “Ratings” herein.
$250,585,000 OBLIGATIONS, SERIES 2025
JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
HUDSON COUNTY, NEW JERSEY
consisting of 
$87,370,000 Water Project Notes, Series 2025A,
$8,925,000 Water Project Notes, Series 2025B (Federally Taxable),
$17,820,000 Sewer Project Notes, Series 2025C, 
$48,065,000 Water Revenue Bonds, Series 2025D,
$78,405,000 Sewer Revenue Bonds, Series 2025E, and
$10,000,000 Water Franchise Revenue Bonds, Series 2025F (Federally Taxable)
Dated:  Date of Delivery Due:  as shown on inside cover
 The $250,585,000 Obligations, Series 2025, consisting of: (i) $87,370,000 Water Project Notes, Series 2025A (the “Tax-Exempt Water Notes”); (ii) $8,925,000 Water Project Notes, Series 2025B (Federally Taxable) (the “Taxable Water Notes” and, 
together with the Tax-Exempt Water Notes, the “Water Notes”), (iii) $17,820,000 Sewer Project Notes, Series 2025C (the “Sewer Notes” and, together with the Water Notes, the “Notes”); (iv) $48,065,000 Water Revenue Bonds, Series 2025D (the “Water 
Bonds”), (v) $78,405,000 Sewer Revenue Bonds, Series 2025E (the “Sewer Bonds” and, together with the Water Bonds, the “Tax-Exempt Bonds” and, together with the Tax-Exempt Water Notes and the Sewer Notes, the “Tax-Exempt Obligations”); and 
(vi) $10,000,000 Water Franchise Revenue Bonds, Series 2025F (Federally Taxable) (the “Taxable Bonds” and, together with the Tax-Exempt Bonds, the “Bonds” and, together with the Notes, the “Obligations”) described herein are being issued by the 
Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority (the “Authority”), a public body corporate and politic of the State of New Jersey (the “State”), and will be issued pursuant to the Municipal and County Utilities Authorities Law, constituting Chapter 183 of the 
Pamphlet Laws of 1957, of the State of New Jersey, and the acts amendatory thereof and supplemental thereto (the “Act”), and the resolutions of the Authority described herein.  
 The Tax Exempt Water Notes are authorized to be issued pursuant to a resolution entitled, “Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Water Project Notes of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” duly adopted by the Authority on  
April 28, 2011, as amended and supplemented (the “General Water Note Resolution”), including by a resolution entitled, “Supplemental Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Not to Exceed $157,000,000 Water Revenue Bonds, and in Anticipation Thereof, 
Project Notes, of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” (the “2023 Supplemental Water Resolution”), duly adopted by the Authority on October 26, 2023 and published as required by law, as further amended and supplemented by a resolution 
entitled, “Supplemental Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Water Revenue Bonds, and in Anticipation Thereof, Project Notes, of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” (the “2024 Supplemental Water Resolution”), duly adopted by the Authority 
on November 25, 2024 and published as required by law, and as further amended and supplemented by an Award Certificate pertaining to the sale of the Water Notes executed by the Authority to be dated the date of issuance of the Water Notes (the  
“Tax-Exempt Water Note Award Certificate” and, together with the General Water Note Resolution, the 2023 Supplemental Water Resolution and the 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution, the “Tax-Exempt Water Note Resolution”).
 The Taxable Water Notes are authorized to be issued pursuant to the General Water Note Resolution, as amended and supplemented by the 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution, and as further amended and supplemented by an Award Certificate 
pertaining to the sale of the Taxable Water Notes executed by the Authority to be dated the date of issuance of the Taxable Water Notes (the “Taxable Water Note Award Certificate” and, together with the General Water Note Resolution and the 2024 
Supplemental Water Resolution, the “Taxable Water Note Resolution” and, together with the Tax/Exempt Water Note Resolution, the “Water Note Resolution”). 
 The Sewer Notes are authorized to be issued pursuant to a resolution entitled, “Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Project Notes of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” duly adopted by the Authority on May 28, 1998, as amended 
and supplemented (the “General Sewer Note Resolution”), including by a resolution entitled, “Supplemental Bond Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Not to Exceed $20,000,000 Sewer Revenue Bonds, and in Anticipation Thereof, Project Notes, of 
the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” (the “Supplemental Sewer Note Resolution”), duly adopted by the Authority on March 27, 2025 and published as required by law, and as further amended and supplemented by an Award Certificate pertaining 
to the sale of the Sewer Notes executed by the Authority to be dated the date of issuance of the Sewer Notes (the “Sewer Award Certificate” and, together with the General Sewer Note Resolution and the Supplemental Sewer Note Resolution, the “Sewer 
Note Resolution”).
 The Water Bonds are authorized to be issued pursuant to a resolution entitled, “Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Revenue Bonds of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” adopted on January 28, 1998, as amended and supplemented (the 
“General Bond Resolution”), including by a resolution entitled, “Supplemental Bond Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Not to Exceed $50,000,000 Water Revenue Bonds, Series 2025 of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” (the “Supplemental 
Water Bond Resolution”), duly adopted by the Authority on March 27, 2025 and published as required by law, and as further amended and supplemented by an Award Certificate pertaining to the sale of the Water Bonds executed by the Authority to be 
dated the date of issuance of the Water Bonds (the “Water Bonds Award Certificate” and, together with the General Bond Resolution and the Supplemental Water Bond Resolution, the “Tax-Exempt Water Bond Resolution”).
 The Sewer Bonds are authorized to be issued pursuant to the General Bond Resolution, as amended and supplemented by a resolution entitled, “Supplemental Bond Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Not to Exceed $80,000,000 Sewer Revenue 
Bonds, Series 2025 of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” (the “Supplemental Sewer Bond Resolution”), duly adopted by the Authority on March 27, 2025 and published as required by law, and as further amended and supplemented by an Award 
Certificate pertaining to the sale of the Sewer Bonds executed by the Authority to be dated the date of issuance of the Sewer Bonds (the “Sewer Bonds Award Certificate” and, together with the General Bond Resolution and the Supplemental Sewer Bond 
Resolution, the “Sewer Bond Resolution”).
 The Taxable Bonds are authorized to be issued pursuant to the General Bond Resolution, as most recently amended and supplemented by the 2023 Supplemental Water Resolution, and as further amended and supplemented by an Award Certificate 
pertaining to the sale of the Taxable Bonds executed by the Authority to be dated the date of issuance of the Taxable Bonds (the “Taxable Bonds Award Certificate” and, together with the General Bond Resolution and the 2023 Supplemental Water Resolution, 
the “Taxable Water Bond Resolution” and, together with the Tax-Exempt Water Bond Resolution, the “Water Bond Resolution”).
 The Water Bond Resolution and the Sewer Bond Resolution are hereinafter collectively referred to as the “Bond Resolution”.  The Water Note Resolution and the Water Bond Resolution are hereinafter collectively referred to as the “Water 
Resolution”.  The Sewer Note Resolution and the Sewer Bond Resolution are hereinafter collectively referred to as the “Sewer Resolution”.  
 The Water Notes are being issued to: (i) obtain new money as set forth herein for the costs of the planning, design, acquisition, improvement, renovation, replacement, construction, and installation of various capital projects to and for the Water 
System (defined as the “System” in the Water Note Resolution) as set forth herein; and (ii) pay capitalized interest on the Water Notes; and (iii) pay the costs of issuance of the Water Notes. See “PURPOSE OF THE OBLIGATIONS – Water Notes” herein.
 The Sewer Notes are being issued to: (i) obtain new money as set forth herein for the costs of the planning, design, acquisition, improvement, renovation, replacement, construction, and installation of various capital projects to and for the Sewer 
System (defined as the “System” in the Sewer Note Resolution) as set forth herein; and (ii) pay capitalized interest on the Sewer Notes, and (iii) pay the costs of issuance of the Sewer Notes. See “PURPOSE OF THE OBLIGATIONS – Sewer Notes” herein. 
 The Water Bonds are being issued to: (i) currently refund the Authority’s $25,000,000 Water Project Notes, Series 2024A, dated April 26, 2024 and maturing on May 1, 2025 (the “Series 2024A Water Notes”); (ii) currently refund the Authority’s 
$25,000,000 Water Project Note, Series 2024E, dated December 30, 2024 and maturing on May 1, 2025 (the “Series 2024E Water Notes); (iii) fund a deposit to the Water Bond Reserve Fund (as defined in the Bond Resolution) for the Water Bonds, and 
(iv) pay the costs of issuance of the Water Bonds. See “PURPOSE OF THE OBLIGATIONS – Water Bonds” herein. 
 The Sewer Bonds are being issued to: (i) currently  refund the Authority’s outstanding $80,000,000 Sewer Project Notes, Series 2024B, dated April 26, 2024 and maturing on May 1, 2025 (the “2024 Sewer Notes”); (ii) fund a deposit to the Sewer 
Bond Reserve Fund (as defined in the Bond Resolution) for the Sewer Bonds, and (iii) pay the costs of issuance of the Sewer Bonds. See “PURPOSE OF THE OBLIGATIONS – Sewer Bonds” herein.
 The Taxable Bonds are being issued to (i) finance a portion of the Authority’s cost of the acquisition and/or extension of the Authority’s right to operate the City of Jersey City (the “City”) water system, including all permits, licenses approvals, 
contracts, revenues and all other related rights associated therewith, pursuant to and in accordance with the Water Service Contract (as defined herein); (ii) fund a deposit to the Water Bond Reserve Fund for the Taxable Bonds, and (iii) pay the costs of 
issuance of the Taxable Bonds. See “PURPOSE OF THE OBLIGATIONS – Taxable Bonds” herein. 
 The Obligations of each series will be issued as fully registered obligations in denominations of $5,000 or any integral multiple of $1,000 in excess thereof.  Upon initial issuance, the Obligations will be registered in the name of and held by Cede & 
Co., as nominee for The Depository Trust Company, New York, New York (“DTC”), an automated depository for securities and clearinghouse for securities transactions.  Purchasers of the Obligations will not receive certificates representing their ownership 
interest in the Obligations so purchased.  See “DESCRIPTION OF THE OBLIGATIONS - Book-Entry Only System” herein.
 Manufacturers and Traders Trust Company, Iselin, New Jersey (the “Trustee”), will act as trustee, paying agent, and registrar for the Obligations.  The Obligations are initially dated the date of delivery and will mature as shown on the inside front 
cover page of this Official Statement.  Interest on the Obligations is calculated on the basis of twelve (12), thirty (30) day months in a three hundred sixty (360) day year.  Interest on the Bonds is payable semiannually on each April 15 and October 15 
commencing on October 15, 2025. Interest on the Notes is payable at maturity on May 1, 2026.  Interest on the Bonds will be credited to the Participants of DTC as listed on the records of DTC as of each preceding April 1 and October 1 (the “Record 
Dates”) for the payment of interest on the Bonds. Interest on the Notes will be credited to the participants of DTC as listed on the records of DTC as of one business day prior to maturity. Provided DTC or its nominee Cede & Co. is the registered owner 
of the Obligations, payments of the principal of and interest on the Obligations will be made directly to DTC or its nominee, which is obligated to remit such principal and interest to DTC Direct Participants, as defined herein.  DTC Direct Participants and 
Indirect Participants, as defined herein, will be responsible for remitting such payments to the beneficial owners of the Obligations. See “DESCRIPTION OF THE OBLIGA
 TIONS – Book-Entry Only System” herein.  
 The scheduled payment of principal of and interest on the Bonds when due will be guaranteed under a municipal bond insurance policy to be issued concurrently with the delivery of the Bonds by Build America Mutual Assurance Company. The 
Water Bonds and the Sewer Bonds have further been designated by Build America Mutual Assurance Company as GreenStar Bonds. See “BOND INSURANCE” herein. 
 The Notes shall not be subject to redemption prior to their stated maturity dates. The Bonds shall be subject to redemption as described more fully herein.  See “DESCRIPTION OF THE OBLIGA TIONS – Redemption” herein
 THE OBLIGATIONS SHALL NOT BE IN ANY WAY A DEBT OR LIABILITY OF THE STATE, THE CITY OF JERSEY CITY (THE “CITY”) (EXCEPT TO THE EXTENT SET FORTH IN THE SEWER SERVICE CONTRACT 
(AS DEFINED HEREIN), AS APPLICABLE, OR THE WATER SERVICE CONTRACT, AS APPLICABLE), OR OF ANY POLITICAL SUBDIVISION THEREOF OTHER THAN THE AUTHORITY, AND SHALL NOT CREATE OR 
CONSTITUTE ANY INDEBTEDNESS, LIABILITY OR OBLIGATION OF THE STATE, THE CITY (EXCEPT TO THE EXTENT SET FORTH IN THE SEWER SERVICE CONTRACT, AS APPLICABLE, OR THE WATER SERVICE 
CONTRACT, AS APPLICABLE) OR OF ANY POLITICAL SUBDIVISION THEREOF OTHER THAN THE AUTHORITY OR BE OR CONSTITUTE A PLEDGE OF THE FAITH AND CREDIT OF THE STATE, THE CITY (EXCEPT 
TO THE EXTENT SET FORTH IN THE SEWER SERVICE CONTRACT, AS APPLICABLE, OR THE WATER SERVICE CONTRACT, AS APPLICABLE) OR OF ANY POLITICAL SUBDIVISION THEREOF.  NEITHER THE STATE, 
THE CITY (EXCEPT TO THE EXTENT SET FORTH IN THE SEWER SERVICE CONTRACT, AS APPLICABLE, OR THE WATER SERVICE CONTRACT, AS APPLICABLE) NOR ANY POLITICAL SUBDIVISION THEREOF 
OTHER THAN THE AUTHORITY IS OBLIGATED TO PAY THE PRINCIPAL OF OR INTEREST ON THE OBLIGATIONS AND NEITHER THE FAITH AND CREDIT NOR THE TAXING POWER OF THE STATE, THE CITY 
(EXCEPT TO THE EXTENT SET FORTH IN THE SEWER SERVICE CONTRACT, AS APPLICABLE, OR THE WATER SERVICE CONTRACT, AS APPLICABLE) OR ANY POLITICAL SUBDIVISION THEREOF, IS PLEDGED 
TO THE PAYMENT OF THE PRINCIPAL OF OR INTEREST ON THE OBLIGATIONS.  THE AUTHORITY HAS NO TAXING POWER.

No dealer, broker, salesperson or any other person has been authorized by the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority 
(the “Authority”) or the City of Jersey City, in the County of Hudson, New Jersey (the “City”) to give any information 
or to make any representations, other than those contained in this Official Statement, and, if given or made, such other 
information or representations must not be relied upon as ha ving been authorized by the foregoing. The information 
contained herein has been provided by the Authority and other sources deemed reliable; however, no representation 
or warranty is made as to its accuracy or completeness and such information is not to be construed as a representation 
or warranty by the Underwriter or, as to information from sources other than itself, by the Authority.  The information 
and expressions of opinion herein are subject to change without notice, and neither the delivery of this Official 
Statement nor any sale hereunder shall, under any circumstances, create any implication that there has been no change 
in any of the information herein since the date hereof, or the date as of which such information is given, if earlier. 
 
References in this Official Statement to laws, rules, regulations, resolutions, agreements, reports and documents do 
not purport to be comprehensive or definitive.  All refere nces to such documents are qualified in their entirety by 
reference to the particular document, the full text of whic h may be inspected at the offi ces of the Authority during 
normal business hours. 
 
This Official Statement does not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy, nor shall there be any 
sale of the Obligations in any jurisdiction in which it is unlawful for any person to make such an offer, solicitation or 
sale.  No dealer, broker, salesman or other person has been authorized to give any information or to make any 
representations other than as contained in this Official Statement.  If given or made, such other information or 
representations must not be relied upon as having been authorized by the Authority or Underwriter. 
 
Upon issuance, the Obligations will not be registered under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended.  The Obligations 
will not be listed on any stock or other securities exchange.  Neither the Securities and Exchange Commission nor any 
other federal, state, municipal or other governmental entity, other than the Authority, will have passed upon the 
accuracy or adequacy of this Official Statement at the time the Obligations are issued. 
 
Certain demographic and financial information concerning th e City is set forth in this Official Statement.  The 
Underwriter has not confirmed the accur acy or completeness of information re lating to the City, and disclaims any 
responsibility for the accuracy or completeness thereof. 
 
This Official Statement includes the cover page hereof and the appendices attached hereto.  The Underwriter has been 
authorized by the Authority to imprint its name on the cover page, together with the Obligations’ offering prices and 
the interest rates per annum for the Obligations on the inside front cover.

The Sewer Bonds maturing on or after October 15, 2036 are redeemable at the option of the Authority, in whole or in

OFFICIAL STATEMENT 
 
$250,585,000 OBLIGATIONS, SERIES 2025 
 
JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
HUDSON COUNTY, NEW JERSEY 
 
consisting of  
 
$87,370,000 Water Project Notes, Series 2025A, 
$8,925,000 Water Project Notes, Series 2025B (Federally Taxable), 
$17,820,000 Sewer Project Notes, Series 2025C,  
$48,065,000 Water Revenue Bonds, Series 2025D 
$78,405,000 Sewer Revenue Bonds, Series 2025E, and 
$10,000,000 Water Franchise Revenue Bonds, Series 2025F (Federally Taxable) 
 
INTRODUCTION 
 
This Official Statement, which includes the front cover page hereof, inside front cover page hereof, 
and the Appendices attached hereto, is furnished by the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority 
(the “Authority”), a public body corporate and politic of the State of New Jersey (the “State”), to 
provide certain information relating to the Author ity, the City of Jersey City, in the County of 
Hudson, New Jersey (the “City”) and the $250,585,000 Obligations, Series 2025 consisting of: (i) 
$87,370,000 Water Project Notes, Series 2025A (the “Tax-Exempt Water Notes”); (ii) $8,925,000 
Water Project Notes, Series 2025B (Federally Taxable) (the “Taxable Water Notes” and, together 
with the Tax-Exempt Water Notes, the “Water  Notes”); (iii) $17,820,000 Sewer Project Notes, 
Series 2025C (the “Sewer Notes”  and, together with the Wate r Notes, the “Notes”); (iv) 
$48,065,000 Water Revenue Bonds, Series 2025D (the “Water Bonds”); (v) $78,405,000 Sewer 
Revenue Bonds, Series 2025E (the “Sewer Bonds” and, together with the Water Bonds, the “Tax-
Exempt Bonds”, and together with the Tax-Exem pt Water Notes and the Sewer Notes, the “Tax-
Exempt Obligations”); and (vi) $10,000,000 Water Franchise Revenue Bonds, Series 2025F 
(Federally Taxable) (the “Taxable Bonds” and, together with the Tax-Exempt Bonds, the “Bonds”, 
and together with the Notes, the “Obligations”), to be issued by the Authority. The Obligations are 
issued pursuant to the Municipal and County U tilities Authorities Law, constituting Chapter 183 
of the Pamphlet Laws of 1957, of the State of Ne w Jersey, and the acts amendatory thereof and 
supplemental thereto (the “Act”), and the resolutions of the Authority described herein. 
 
Manufacturers and Traders Trust Company, Iselin, New Jersey, has been appointed to serve as 
trustee, paying agent, and registrar for the Obligations (“Trustee”, “Paying Agent”, and 
“Registrar”). Copies of the Sewer Resolution and the Water Resolution (each as defined herein), 
that certain Sewer Service Cont ract by and between the Authority and the City dated as of 
December 1, 1985, as amended  May 1, 2003 (the “Sewer Service Contract ”), and that certain 
Amended and Restated Water Services Franch ise and Service Agreement by and between the 
Authority and the City dated as of February 1, 2024 (the “Water Service Contract”) are attached 
hereto as Appendices D, E, F and G, respectively, are also on file at the offices of the Authority in 
Jersey City, New Jersey, at the offices of the City in Jersey City, New Jersey, and at the corporate 
trust office of the Trustee in Iselin, New Jersey . Reference is made to such documents for the  
2 
  
 
 
provisions relating to, among other things, the terms of and the se curity for the Obligations, the 
custody and application of the proceeds of the Obligations, the rights and remedies of the holders 
of the Obligations, and the rights, duties and obligations of the Authority, the City and the Trustee. 
 
There follows in this Official Statement brief descriptions of the Obligations, the Sewer 
Resolution, the Water Resolution, the Sewer Service Contract, th e Water Service Contract, the 
Authority, the City, the Sewer System and the Water System. Certain financial information relating 
to the Authority is attached to this Official Statement as Appendix A. Certain demographic and 
financial information relating to the City is atta ched to this Official Statement as Appendices B 
and C, respectively. With regard to the information provided by the City, such information has not 
been confirmed by the Authority as  to its accuracy or completeness of such information relating 
to the City and the City disclaims any responsib ility for the accuracy or completeness thereof.  
Capitalized words and terms which are used herein, which are not ordinarily capitalized and which 
are not otherwise defined herein, shall have the meanings which are assigned to such words and 
terms in the Sewer Resolution and/or the Water Re solution.  The summaries of and references to 
all documents, statutes, reports, and other instruments which are referred to herein do not purport 
to be complete, comprehensive or definitive, and each such summary and reference is qualified in 
its entirety by reference to such document, statute, report or instrument. 
 
AUTHORIZATION FOR THE OBLIGATIONS 
 
The Tax Exempt Water Notes are authorized to be issued pursuant to a resolution entitled, 
“Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Water Project Notes of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities 
Authority” duly adopted by the Authority on Apr il 28, 2011, as amended and supplemented (the 
“General Water Note Resolution”), including by a resolution entitled, “Supplemental Resolution 
Authorizing the Issuance of Not to Exceed $157,000,000 Water Revenue Bonds, and in 
Anticipation Thereof, Project Notes, of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” (the “2023 
Supplemental Water Resolution”), duly adopt ed by the Authority on October 26, 2023 and 
published as required by law, as further amende d and supplemented by a resolution entitled, 
“Supplemental Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Water Revenue Bonds, and in Anticipation 
Thereof, Project Notes, of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” (the “2024 Supplemental 
Water Resolution”), duly adopted by the Authority on November 25, 2024 and published as 
required by law, and as further amended and supplemented by an Award Certificate pertaining to 
the sale of the Water Notes executed by the Authority to be dated the date of issuance of the Water 
Notes (the “Tax-Exempt Water Note Award Certificate” and, together with the General Water 
Note Resolution, the 2023 Supplemental Water Resolution and the 2024 Supplemental Water 
Resolution, the “Tax-Exempt Water Note Resolution”). 
 
The Taxable Water Notes are authorized to be issued pursuant to the General Water Note 
Resolution, as amended and supplemented by the 2024 Supplemental Water  Resolution, and as 
further amended and supplemented by an Award Certificate pertaining to the sale of the Taxable 
Water Notes executed by the Authority to be dated the date of issuance of the Taxable Water Notes 
(the “Taxable Water Note Award Certificate”  and, together with the General Water Note  
3 
  
 
 
Resolution and the 2024 Supplemental Water Resolu tion, the “Taxable Water Note Resolution” 
and, together with the Tax-Exempt Water Note Resolution, the “Water Note Resolution”).  
 
The Sewer Notes are authorized to be issued pursuant to a resolution  entitled, “Resolution 
Authorizing the Issuance of Project Notes of th e Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” duly 
adopted by the Authority on May 28, 1998, as amended and supplemented (the “General Sewer 
Note Resolution”), including by a resolution entitled, “Supplemental Bond Resolution Authorizing 
the Issuance of Not to Exceed $20,000,000 Sewer Revenue Bonds, and in Anticipation Thereof, 
Project Notes, of the Jersey City Municipal Ut ilities Authority” (the “Supplemental Sewer Note 
Resolution”), duly adopted by the Authority on March 27, 2025 and published as required by law, 
and as further amended and suppl emented by an Award Certificate pertaining to th e sale of the 
Sewer Notes executed by the Authority to be dated the date of issuance of the Sewer Notes (the 
“Sewer Award Certificate” a nd, together with the General Sewer Note Resolution and the 
Supplemental Sewer Note Resolution, the “Sewer Note Resolution”). 
 
The Water Bonds are authorized to be issued pursuant to a re solution entitled, “Resolution 
Authorizing the Issuance of Revenue Bonds of the Jersey City Munici pal Utilities Authority” 
adopted on January 28, 1998, as amended and suppl emented (the “General  Bond Resolution”), 
including by a resolution entitle d, “Supplemental Bond Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of 
Not to Exceed $50,000,000 Water Revenue Bonds, Se ries 2025 of the Jersey City Municipal 
Utilities Authority” (the “Supplemental Water Bond Resolution”), duly adopted by the Authority 
on March 27, 2025 and published as required by law, and as furt her amended and supplemented 
by an Award Certificate pertaining to the sale of the Water Bonds executed by the Authority to be 
dated the date of issuance of the Water Bonds (the “Water Bonds Award Certificate” and, together 
with the General Bond Resolution and the Supplemental Water Bond Resolution, the “Tax-Exempt 
Water Bond Resolution”). 
 
The Sewer Bonds are authorized to be issued pursuant to the General Bond Resolution, as amended 
and supplemented by a resolution entitled, “S upplemental Bond Resolution Authorizing the 
Issuance of Not to Exceed $80,000,000 Sewer Revenue Bonds, Series 2025 of the Jersey City 
Municipal Utilities Authority” (the “Supplemental Sewer Bond Resolution”), duly adopted by the 
Authority on March 27, 2025 and published as required by law, and as further amended and 
supplemented by an Award Certificate pertaining to the sale of the Sewer Bonds executed by the 
Authority to be dated the date of issuance of the Sewer Bonds (the “Sewer Bonds Award 
Certificate” and, together with  the General Bond Resolution a nd the Supplemental Sewer Bond 
Resolution, the “Sewer Bond Resolution”). 
 
The Taxable Bonds are authorized to be issued pursuant to the General Bond Resolution, as most 
recently amended and supplemented by the 2023 S upplemental Water Resolution, and as further 
amended and supplemented by an Awa rd Certificate pertaining to th e sale of the Taxable Bonds 
executed by the Authority to be dated the date of issuance of the Taxabl e Bonds (the “Taxable 
Bonds Award Certificate” and, together with the General Bond Resolution and the 2023 
Supplemental Water Resolution, the “Taxable Water Bond Resolution” and, together with the Tax-
Exempt Water Bond Resolution, the “Water Bond Resolution”).

The Water Bond Resolution and the Sewer Bond Reso lution are hereinafter collectively referred 
to as the “Bond Resolution”.  The Water Note  Resolution and the Water Bond Resolution are 
hereinafter collectively referred to as the “Water Resolution”.  The Sewer Note Resolution and the 
Sewer Bond Resolution are hereinafter collectively referred to as the “Sewer Resolution”.   
 
DESCRIPTION OF THE OBLIGATIONS 
 
General 
 
Each series of Notes shall be issued in the respective principal amounts set forth on the inside 
cover page hereof, shall each be dated their respective dates of delivery, and shall each bear interest 
at the respective rates set forth in the inside cover page hereof, from the dated date to maturity. 
Interest on the Notes is payable at maturity. 
 
Each series of Bonds shall be issued in the respective principa l amounts set fort h on the inside 
cover page hereof, shall each be dated their re spective dates of delivery and will mature on the 
respective dates and in the respect ive amounts and will bear interest payable semiannually as set 
forth on the inside cover page here of. Interest on the Bonds is ca lculated on the ba sis of twelve 
(12), thirty (30) day months in a three hundred sixty (360) day year. 
 
Upon initial issuance and delivery, the Obligations will be registered in the name of Cede & Co., 
as nominee of The Depository Trust Company, New York, New York (“DTC”), which will act as 
securities depository for the Obligations.  In terest on the Bonds will be credited to DTC 
Participants, as defined below, as listed on the records of DTC as of each preceding April 1 and 
October 1 (the “Record Dates”) with  respect to the payment of inte rest on the Bonds. Interest on 
the Notes will be credited to the participants of DTC as listed on the records of DTC as of one 
business day prior to ma turity.  The Obligations may be purchased in book-entry form in 
authorized denominations of $5,000 or any integral multiple of $1,000 in excess thereof, through 
book entries made on the books of DTC. 
 
So long as DTC or its nominee, Cede & Co., is th e registered owner of the Obligations, payment 
of the principal of and interest on the Obligations will be made directly to Cede & Co., as nominee 
of DTC. Disbursement of such payments to the participants of DTC (“DTC Participants”) is the 
responsibility of DTC and disburse ments of such payments to th e Beneficial Owners (as defined 
herein) of the Obligations is the responsibility of the DTC Participants and not the Authority or 
the Trustee.  See “Book Entry Only System” herein. 
 
Redemption 
 
The Notes  
 
The Notes shall not be subject to redemption prior to their stated maturity dates.  
 
   
5 
  
 
 
The Bonds 
 
 Optional Redemption 
 
The Bonds maturing before Octobe r 15, 2036 are not subject to redemption prior to their stated 
maturities. The Bonds maturing on and after October 15, 2036 are redeemable at the option of the 
Authority, in whole or in part , on any date on or after Oct ober 15, 2035, at a redemption price 
equal to 100% of the principal amount thereof (the “Redemption Price”), plus in each case accrued 
interest to the date fixed for redemption. 
 
Mandatory Sinking Fund Redemption 
 
 
Water Bonds 
 
The Water Bonds maturing on October 15, 2048 shall be subject to mandatory sinking fund 
redemption by lot, at a redemption price equal to  100% of the principal amount to be redeemed, 
plus accrued interest to the redemption date on  October 15 in each of the years and principal 
amounts set forth below:

8 
  
 
 
The Trustee shall, at least thirty (30) days and not more than forty-five (45) days prior to the date 
fixed for redemption, mail by first class mail, postage prepaid, to the registered owners of the 
Bonds to be redeemed (provided that such notice period may be wa ived by the Bond Holders) at 
their addresses as the same shall appear, if at all, upon the registry books of the Trustee, a notice 
to the effect that the Authority has elected to red eem all the Bonds or a part  thereof, as the case 
may be, on a date therein designated,  specifying, in the case of the redemption of less than all of 
the outstanding Bonds, the principal amount of the Bonds to be redeemed and the portion, if less 
than all, of any Bond to be redeemed, and in every case stating that on said date there will become 
and be due and payable upon each Bond so to be redeemed, at the designated office of the Trustee, 
the principal thereof, together with the accrued interest to such date, with such premium, if any, as 
is due and payable on such Bond upon such redemption, and that from and after such date interest 
thereon will cease to accrue.  At any time the B onds are held by DTC, the Trustee shall send any 
notice of redemption to DTC at such address as may be given by DTC in writing to the Trustee.  
The foregoing notice may be sent by legible facs imile or by other secure method which enables 
the Trustee to verify the submission of such notice. 
 
If at the time of mailing of any notice of redemp tion there shall not have been deposited with the 
Trustee moneys sufficient to redeem all the Bo nds called for redemption, such notice shall state 
that it is subject to the deposit of the redemption moneys with the Trustee not later than the opening 
of business on the redemption date and shall be of no effect unless such are so deposited. 
 
Book-Entry Only System  
 
DTC will act as securities depository for the Obliga tions. The Obligations of each series will be 
issued as fully-registered securi ties registered in the name of  Cede & Co. (DTC's partnership 
nominee) or such other name as may be requested by an authori zed representative of DTC. One 
fully-registered note or bond certificate, as applicable, will be issued fo r each series of the 
Obligations, in the aggregate principal amount of  each maturity of such series, and will be 
deposited with DTC. 
DTC, the world’s largest depository, is a limited-purpose trust company organized under the New 
York Banking Law, a “banking organization” within the meaning of the New York Banking Law, 
a member of the Federal Reserve System, a “clearing corporation” within the meaning of the New 
York Uniform Commercial Code, and a “clearing agency” registered pursuant to the provisions of 
Section 17A of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.  DTC holds and provides asset servicing for 
over 3.5 million issues of U.S. and non-U.S. equity issues, cor porate and municipal debt issues, 
and money market instruments (from over 100 c ountries) that DTC’s participants (“Direct 
Participants”) deposit w ith DTC.  DTC also facilitates th e post-trade settlement among Direct 
Participants of sales and other securities transactions in deposited securities, through electronic 
computerized book-entry transfers and pledges between Direct Participants’ accounts.  This 
eliminates the need for physical mo vement of securities ce rtificates.  Direct Participants include 
both U.S. and non-U.S. securities brokers and dealers, banks, trust companies, clearing 
corporations, and certain other organizations.  DTC is a wholly-owned subsidiary of The 
Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (“DTCC”).  DTCC is the holding company for DTC, 
National Securities Clearing Corporation and Fixed Income Clearing Corporation, all of which are 
registered clearing agencies.  DTCC is owned by the users of its regulated subsidiaries.  Access to  
9 
  
 
 
the DTC system is also available to others such as both U.S. a nd non-U.S. securities brokers and 
dealers, banks and trust companies, and clearing corporations th at clear through or maintain a 
custodial relationship with  a Direct Participant, either di rectly or indirectly (“Indirect 
Participants”).  DTC has Standard & Poor’s ra ting of AA+. The DTC Rules applicable to its 
Participants are on file with the Securities and Exchange Commission.  More information about 
DTC can be found at www.dtcc.com.  
Purchases of the Obligations under the DTC system must be made by or through Direct 
Participants, which will receive a credit for the Obligations on DTC's records. The ownership 
interest of each actual purchaser of each series of the Obligations (“Beneficial Owner”) is in turn 
to be recorded on the Direct and Indirect Participants’ records. Beneficial Owners will not receive 
written confirmation from DTC of their purchase.  Beneficial Owners are,  however, expected to 
receive written confirmations provi ding details of the transaction, as  well as periodic statements 
of their holdings, from the Direct or Indirect Participant through which the Beneficial Owner 
entered into the transaction. Transfers of ownership interests in each series of the Obligations are 
to be accomplished by entries made on the books of Direct and Indirect Participants acting on 
behalf of Beneficial Owners. Beneficial Owners will not receive certifi cates representing their 
ownership interests in each series of the Obligations, except in the event that use of the book-entry 
system for each series of the Obligations is discontinued. 
To facilitate subsequent transfers, all Obligation certificates deposited by Direct Participants with 
DTC are registered in the name of DTC’s partnership nominee, Ce de & Co., or such other name 
as may be requested by an authorized represen tative of DTC.  The deposit of each series of  
Obligations with DTC and their registration in the name of Cede & Co. or such other DTC nominee 
do not affect any change in beneficial ownership. DTC has no knowledge of the actual Beneficial 
Owners of the Obligations; DTC’s records reflect onl y the identity of the Direct Participants to 
whose accounts such Obligations ar e credited, which may or may no t be the Beneficial Owners. 
The Direct and Indirect Participants will remain responsible for keeping account of their holdings 
on behalf of their customers. 
Conveyance of notices and other communications  by DTC to Direct Participants, by Direct 
Participants to Indirect Participants, and by Dir ect Participants and Indirect Participants to 
Beneficial Owners of each series  of the Obligations will be  governed by arrangements among 
them, subject to any statutory or regulatory requirements as may be in effect from time to time.  
Beneficial Owners of each series of the Obligati ons may wish to take certain steps to augment 
transmission to them of notices of significant ev ents with respect to the Obligations, such as 
redemptions, tenders, defaults and proposed amendments to the bond and/or note documents.  For 
example, Beneficial Owners of each series of the Obligations may wish to ascertain that the 
nominee holding the respective seri es of Obligations for their be nefit has agreed to obtain and 
transmit notices to Beneficial Owners.  In the alternative, Beneficial Owners may wish to provide 
their names and addresses to the registrar and request that copies of the notices be provided directly 
to them. 
Neither DTC nor Cede & Co. (nor any other DTC nominee) will consent or vote with respect to 
each series of the Obligations unless authorized by a Direct Participant in accordance with DTC’s  
10 
  
 
 
Procedures. Under its usual procedures, DTC mails an Omnibus Proxy to the Authority as soon as 
possible after the record date. The Omnibus Proxy assigns Cede & Co.’s consenting or voting 
rights to those Direct Participants to whose accounts each series of the Obligations are credited on 
the record date (identified in a listing attached to the Omnibus Proxy). 
Redemption proceeds and principal and interest pa yments on each series of the Obligations, as 
applicable, will be made to Cede & Co., or such other nominee as may be requested by an 
authorized representative of DTC. DTC’s practice is to credit Direct Participants’ accounts upon 
DTC’s receipt of funds and corresponding detail information from the Authority or Trustee, on the 
payable date in accordance with their respective holdings shown on DTC’s records.  Payments by 
Participants to Beneficial Owners will be gov erned by standing instructions and customary 
practices, as is the case with securities held fo r the accounts of customers in bearer form or 
registered in “street name,” and will be the responsibility of such Participant and not of DTC, the 
Trustee or the Authority, subject to any statutory or regulatory re quirements as may be in effect 
from time to time. Payment of redemption proceed s, if applicable, and principal and interest 
payments to Cede & Co. (or such other nominee as may be requested by an authorized 
representative of DTC) is the responsibility of the Authority or Trustee, disbursement of such 
payments to Direct Participants will be the re sponsibility of DTC, and disbursement of such 
payments to the Beneficial Owners will be the responsibility of Direct and Indirect Participants. 
DTC may discontinue providing its services as securities depository with respect to each series of 
the Obligations at any time by giving reasonable notice to the Authority or  Trustee. Under such 
circumstances, in the event that a successor securities depository is not obtained, Bond and/or Note 
certificates, as applicable, are required to be printed and delivered. 
The Authority may decide to di scontinue use of the system of  book-entry only transfers through 
DTC (or a successor securities depository).  In that event, B ond and/or Note certificates, as 
applicable, will be printed and delivered. 
The information in this sect ion concerning DTC and DTC’s book- entry only system has been 
obtained from sources that the Authority believes to be reliable, but the Authority takes no 
responsibility for the accuracy thereof. 
 
Discontinuation of Book-Entry Only System 
 
If the Authority, in its sole discretion, determines that DTC is not capable of discharging its duties, 
or if DTC discontinues providing its services with respect to each series of the Obligations at any 
time, the Authority will attempt to locate another qualified Securities Depository.  If the Authority 
fails to find such a Securities Depository, or if the Authority determines, in its sole discretion, that 
it is in the best interest of the Authority or th at the interest of the Beneficial Owners might be 
adversely affected if the book-entry only system of transfer is continued (the Authority undertakes 
no obligation to make an investigation to determine the occurrence of any events that would permit 
it to make such determination) the Authority shall notify DTC of the termination of the book-entry 
only system. 
  
11 
  
 
 
BOND INSURANCE 
BOND INSURANCE POLICY 
Concurrently with the issuance of the Bonds, Build America Mutual Assurance Company (“BAM”) will 
issue its Municipal Bond Insurance Policy for the Bonds (the “Policy”).  The Policy guarantees the 
scheduled payment of principal of and interest on the Bonds when due as set forth in the form of the Policy 
included as an exhibit to this Official Statement.  
The Policy is not covered by any insurance secu rity or guaranty fund established under New York, 
California, Connecticut or Florida insurance law.   
BUILD AMERICA MUTUAL ASSURANCE COMPANY 
BAM is a New York domiciled mutual insurance corporation and is licensed to conduct financial guaranty 
insurance business in all fifty states of the United States and the District of Columbia.  BAM provides credit 
enhancement products to issuers in the U.S. public finance markets.  BAM will only insure municipal 
bonds, as defined in Section 6901 of the New York Insurance Law, which are most often issued by states, 
political subdivisions, integral parts of states or politi cal subdivisions or entities otherwise eligible for the 
exclusion of income under section 115 of the U.S. Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended.  No member 
of BAM is liable for the obligations of BAM.   
 
The address of the principal executive offices of BAM is: 200 Liberty Street, 27
th Floor, New York, New 
York 10281, its telephone number is: 212-235-2500, and its website is located at: www.bambonds.com.  
 
BAM is licensed and subject to regulation as a financ ial guaranty insurance corporation under the laws of 
the State of New York and in particular Articles 41 and 69 of the New York Insurance Law. 
BAM’s financial strength is rated “AA/Stable” by S&P Global Ratings, a business unit of Standard & Poor's 
Financial Services LLC (“S&P”). An explanation of the significance of the rating and current reports may 
be obtained from S&P at https://www.spglobal.com/en/.   The rating of BAM should be evaluated 
independently. The rating reflects S&P’s current assessment of the creditworthiness of BAM and its ability 
to pay claims on its policies of insurance. The above rating is not a recommendation to buy, sell or hold the 
Bonds, and such rating is subject to revision or withdrawal at any time by S&P, including withdrawal 
initiated at the request of BAM in its sole discretion.   Any downward revision or withdrawal of the above 
rating may have an adverse effect on the market price of the Bonds.  BAM only guarantees scheduled 
principal and scheduled interest payments payable by the issuer of the Bonds on the date(s) when such 
amounts were initially scheduled to become due and pa yable (subject to and in accordance with the terms 
of the Policy), and BAM does not guarantee the market price or liquidity of the Bonds, nor does it guarantee 
that the rating on the Bonds will not be revised or withdrawn. 
Capitalization of BAM 
BAM’s total admitted assets, total liabilities, and total capital and surplus, as of December 31, 2024 and as 
prepared in accordance with statutory accounting practices prescribed or permitted by the New York State 
Department of Financial Services were $498.6 million, $253.4 million and $245.2 million, respectively. 
BAM is party to a first loss reinsurance treaty that provides first loss protection up to a maximum of 15% 
of the par amount outstanding for each policy issued by BAM, subject to certain limitations and restrictions.  12 
BAM’s most recent Statutory Annual Statement, which has been filed with the New York State Insurance 
Department and posted on BAM’s website at www.bambonds.com, is incorporated herein by reference and 
may be obtained, without charge, upon request to BA M at its address provided above (Attention: Finance 
Department).  Future financial statements will similarly be made available when published. 
BAM makes no representation regarding the Bonds or the advisability of investing in the Bonds.  In 
addition, BAM has not independently verified, makes no representation regarding, and does not accept any

BAM GreenStar Bonds 
The Water Bonds and the 
Sewer Bonds have been designated BAM GreenStar Bonds  because BAM has 
determined that the use of bond pro ceeds by the Issuer as described in th is Official Statement and in any 
additional information obtained by BAM aligns with one of the Green Bond Principles (“GBPs”) developed 
by the International Capital Markets Association (“ICMA”).  The GBPs were developed by the ICMA with 
the goal of establishing universally accepted guidelines for the issuance of green bonds, and one of the key 
requirements addresses the use of proceeds.  BAM h as been identified by the ICMA as an observer 
organization that is active in the field of green and/or social or sustainability finance and as a Climate Bond 
Initiative approved verifier.  The GreenStar Credit Pr ofile prepared by BAM for the Water Bonds and the 
Sewer Bonds will identify which of the following GBP categories applies to the Water Bonds and the Sewer 
Bonds:  
 renewable energy
 energy efficiency
 pollution prevention and control
 environmentally sustainable management of living natural resources and land use
 terrestrial and aquatic biodiversity
 clean transportation
 climate change adaptation
 sustainable water and wastewater management
 green buildings
Each of the GBPs correlates to one of the following UN Sustainable Development Goals which will also 
be included in the GreenStar Credit Profile for the Water Bonds and the Sewer Bonds:   
 clean water and sanitation
 affordable and clean energy
 sustainable cities and communities
 industry innovation and infrastructure
 responsible consump
 tion and pro
duction
 climate action
 life below water
 life on land
The Issuer makes no representation regarding the applic ability of or suitability of the GreenStar 
designation. The term “GreenStar” is neither defined in, nor related to, the security documents relating to 
the Water Bonds and the Sewer Bonds. The GreenStar designation is solely for identification purposes and is not intended to provide or imply that the owners of the Water Bonds and the Sewer Bonds are entitled to 
any security other than that described in this official statement.  The Issuer is under no contractual or other 
legal obligation to ensure compliance with any legal or other principles relating to “GreenStar” 
designatio
n. The Issuer has made no commitment to provide ongoing reporting or information regarding 
the designati
on or compliance with the GBPs.

of due

MARKET PROTECTION 
The Authority 
The Authority routinely issues Project Notes pursuant to the General Water Note Resolution and/or 
General Sew
er Note Resolution, and Water Bonds and Sewer Bonds pursuant to the General Bond 
Resolution to the New Jersey Infrastructure Bank and the New Jersey Department of 
Environmental Protection, as appl ic able, to finance eligible cap ital impr ovements to the Water 
System and Sewer System, respectively. The Au thority issued approximately $215.35 million in 
such obligations in 2024 and anticipates issuing approximately $10 million of Project Notes to 
short-term fund additional capital improvements in 2025. 
13 14 
In 2011, the Authority entered into a Consent Decree (No. 90-5-1-1-09499) (the Consent 
Decree) with the United States Environmental Protection Agency (USEPA) to address alleged 
violations of Section 301(a) of the Clean Water Act (CWA), 33 U.S.C. Section 1311(a), and the 
New Jersey Pollutant Discharge Elimination System General Permit for Combined Sewer System 
(CSS General Permit) that was issued to it pursuant to Section 402 of the CWA, 33 U.S.C. 
Section 1342.  On January 27, 2022, the United States Department of Justice and the USEPA 
announced an agreement with the Authority to modify the Consent Decree (the Modification) 
pursuant to a Petition for a Modification of the Consent Decree, to provide a roadmap for 
improvement of the Authoritys Water System and Sewer Systems, which Modification the 
USEPA subsequently granted and the United States District Court for District of New Jersey 
approved. Pursuant to the Consent Decree and the Modification, the Authority anticipates that it 
will have to undertake in excess of $1.23 billion in capital improvements to the Water System and 
Sewer System over the next 7 years. It is expected that the costs of these improvements will be 
funded from available federal and/or State grants and from proceeds of Water Bonds and/or Sewer 
Bonds, as applicable, under the General Bond Resolution, and/or Additional Project Notes under 
the General Water Note Resolution and/or General Sewer Note Resolution, as applicable, to be 
issued from time to time by the Authority.  Such Water Bonds and/or Sewer Bonds, as applicable, 
and/or Additional Project Notes would be secured primarily by revenues of the Authority, 
primarily from user fees, but would be additionally secured by the Water Service Contract and 
Sewer Service Contract, as applicable, by which the City is obligated to make payments to the 
Authority in respect of certain deficits of the Authority, if any. See THE SEWER SYSTEM  
United States Environmental Protection Agency Consent Decree and Modification herein. 
The City 
The City presently expects to issue approximately $43.6 million in Bond Anticipation Notes and 
$36.1 million in Special Emergency Notes in July 2025. 
PURPOSE OF THE OBLIGATIONS 
Water Notes 
The Water Notes are being issued to: (i) obtain new money for the costs of the planning, design, 
acquisition, improvement, renovation, replacement, construction, and installation of one or more 
of the following capital projects to and for the Water System: Boonton Reservoir Project, Aquaduct 
Project, New York Avenue, Princeton Avenue, VanHorne Street, Crosstown Main, Bates Street, 
Bayfront, Canal Crossing, Route 7, Hilltop, Burma Road, Jersey Avenue, Large Valve 
Replacement, New Pressure Reducing Valve, French & Parello Dam Inspections, Baldwin Avenue 
Bridge, Garfield Avenue, Ocean Avenue/Warren Avenue/Academy Water, the Authority Water 
System Capital Improvement Program, Myrtle Avenue Water Replacement, Carteret Avenue, 
Summit & Griffith Water Main, Division Street, 7th Street and 18th and Marin Boulevard, Garfield 
Avenue Emergency Utility Replacement, Hoboken Avenue Emergency Sewer Separation and 
Water Main Replacement, Myrtle Avenue Emergency Sewer & Water Replacement, Brunswick 
Street & 2nd Street Emergency Water Main Relocation & Sewer Replacement, 18th Street & 
Long-Term Authority Financing Plans - United States Environmental Protection Agency 
Consent Decree and Modification 15 
Marin Boulevard Em
ergency Sewer & Water Re placement, Johnston Avenue Emergency Sewer 
and Water Replacement, Spring Street Emergency Water & Sewer Replacement, Baldwin Avenue 
Emergency Water Main & Repair  Replacement, West Side Ave nue Emergency Sewer & Water 
Replacement, Sussex Street Water Main Replacement, South Street Hancock, Summit Avenue & 
Carlton Avenue Aqueduct Improvements, 475 Communipaw, Bates Street Redevelopment Water 
Main Replacement Project, Culver Avenue Wat er Main Replacement, Boonton Reservoir Dam 
Improvements and Various Security Cameras, ISP Circuits, Cabling, Fiber Optic, and other related 
equipment, (ii) pay capitalized interest on the Water Notes; and (iii) pay the costs of issuance of 
the Water Notes.  
For those costs of the identified capital project s set forth above and not otherwise paid from the 
proceeds of the Water Notes, the Authority inte nds to issue Additional Water Bonds and/or 
Additional Project Notes under the Water Resolution, including the financing of eligible projects 
through the NJIB’s financing program.  
Sewer Notes 
The Sewer Notes are being issued  to: (i) obtain new money for the costs of the planning, design, 
acquisition, improvement, renovation, replacement, construction, and installation of one or more 
of the following capital projec ts to and for the Sewer Syste m: Grand St. Sewer Cleaning and 
Inspection, Phase I, Phase 3 & 4 Sewer Rehabil itation, Sixth Street CSO Extension, Claremont 
Carteret Outfall, East Side Plant, Outfall Chambers, Phase V of Combined Sewer, Green 
Infrastructure, 3 Pump Stations / Flood Hardening, Phase 1 & 2 Sewer Rehabilitation, Tree 
Trenches, Van Winkle Sanitary Sewer Rehabilitation, Carteret Avenue 96" Sewer Replacement, 54 
Inch JCMUA/PVSC Forcemain Repair, Phase 2A, 5A & 5B Sewer Rehabilitation, Rehabilitation 
of Van Winkle Outfall, 18
th Street Netting Facility, Sanitary Sewer Main Rehabilitation / Lining, 
Phase 5 Sewer Repairs, Phase 1 and 2 Sewer Reha bilitation, Sanitary Sewer Rehabilitation, Pine 
Street Syphon, Mill Creek Sewer Improvements, Grant Street Sewer Improvements, Phase 5A, 
5B, 6A, 6B, 7A, 7B and 8 Sewer Repairs, Pump Stations 3B, Grit Chamber Improvements and 
New Administration and Operations Building; (ii) pay capitalized interest on the Sewer Notes; and 
(iii) pay the costs of issuance of the Sewer Notes.
For those costs of the identified capital projects not otherwise paid from the proceeds of the Sewer 
Notes, or otherwise paid from the proceeds of  the hereinafter defined 2022 Sewer Notes, the 
Authority intends to issue Additional Sewer B onds and/or Additional Project Notes under the 
Sewer Resolution, including the financing of eligible projects  through the NJIB’s financing 
program.  
Water Bonds 
The Water Bonds are being issued to: (i) currently refund the Au thority’s $25,000,000 Water 
Project Notes, Series 2024A dated April 26, 2024 and maturing on May 1, 2025 (the “Series 2024A 
Water Notes”); (ii) currently refund the Authority’s $25,000,000 Water Project Note, Series 2024E 
dated December 30, 2024 and maturing on May 1, 2025 (the “Series 2024E Water Notes” and,  
16 
  
 
 
together with the Series 2024A Water Notes, the “Prior Water Notes”); (iii) fund a deposit to the 
Water Bond Reserve Fund for the Wat er Bonds; and (iv) pay the co sts of issuance of the Water 
Bonds. The Prior Water Notes were issued to, among other things, prov ide new money for the 
costs of the planning, design, acquisition, improvement, renovation, replacement, construction and 
installation of various capital projects to and for the Water System.  
 
Sewer Bonds 
 
The Sewer Bonds are being issued to: (i) currently refund the Authority’s outstanding $80,000,000 
Sewer Project Notes, Series 2024B, dated April 26, 2024 and maturing on May 1, 2025 (the “2024 
Sewer Notes”); (ii) fund a deposit to the Sewer Bond Reserve Fund for the Sewer Bonds; and (iii) 
pay the costs of issuance of the Sewer Bonds. Th e 2024 Sewer Notes were issued to (i) currently 
refund the Authority’s outstanding $80,000,000 Sewer Project Notes, Series 2023A, dated May 3, 
2023 and maturing on May 3, 2024 (the “2023 Sewer No tes”); and (ii) pay th e costs of issuance 
of the 2024 Sewer Notes. The 2023 Sewer Notes were issued to: (i) currently refund the 
Authority’s $80,000,000 Sewer Project Notes, Series 2022A, dated May 5, 2022 and maturing on 
May 5, 2023 (the “2022 Sewer Notes”), which were issued to pay the costs of the planning, design, 
acquisition, improvement, renovation, replacement, construction and installation of various capital 
projects to and for the Sewer System; and (ii) pay the costs of issuance of the 2023 Sewer Notes.  
 
Taxable Bonds 
 
The Taxable Bonds are being issued to (i) fina nce a portion of the Authority’s cost of the 
acquisition and/or extension of the Authority’s right to operate the City’s Water System, including 
all permits, licenses approvals, contracts, revenues and all other related rights associated therewith, 
pursuant to and in accordance with the Water Service Cont ract, consisting of a $10,000,000 
payment due from the Authority to the City in 2025; (ii) fund a deposit to the Water Bond Reserve 
Fund for the Water Bonds; and (iii) pay the costs of issuance of the Taxable Bonds.

SECURITY FOR THE OBLIGATIONS 
 
Sewer Notes 
 
The Authority is authorized to issue the Sewer Notes pursuant to Section 311 of the General Sewer 
Note Resolution.  The Sewer Notes are direct and special obligations of the Authority, and the full 
faith and credit of the Authority is  pledged to the payment of the principal of and the interest on 
the Sewer Notes as set forth in the General Sewer Note Resolution.  However, the power and the 
obligation of the Authority to cause application of Sewer Revenues, as hereinafter defined, or other 
funds to the payment of the principal of or the interest on the Sewer Notes is subordinate to the 
pledge of Sewer Revenues to the payment of Se wer Operating Expenses, as hereinafter defined 
and to the holders of the First Lien Sewer Bonds, if any, First Lien Water Bonds, if any, and the 
Bonds (each as such terms are defined in the General Bond Resolution).  There are no First Lien 
Sewer Bonds or First Lien Water Bonds outstanding. See “Outstanding Sewer Bonds”, “Authority 
Water Bonds”, and “City Water Bonds” below for a description of the outstanding Sewer Bonds, 
Authority Water Bonds, and City Water Bonds, respectively. 
 
The term “Sewer Revenues” under the General Sewer Note Resolution means all Service Charges 
(as defined in the General Sewer Note Resolution) , fees, rents, charges and other income derived 
or to be derived by the Authority from or for the operation, use or services of the Sewer System.  
19 
  
 
 
 
The term “Sewer Operating Expenses” under the General Sewer Note Resolution (which, by its 
terms, is as defined in the General Bond Resolution), shall mean  the Authority's reasonable and 
necessary current expenses of maintaining, repa iring and operating the Sewer System, including 
without limitation, all administrative, general and commercial expenses, insurance and surety bond 
premiums, payments to others for the collection, treatment or disposal of sewage and other wastes, 
engineering expenses, legal expenses, auditing expenses, payments to pension, retirement, health 
and hospitalization funds, any taxes that may be lawfully imposed on the Authority or its income 
or operations or the property under its control, ordinary and current  rentals of equipment or other 
property, usual expenses of maintenance and repair , costs of service connections from mains to 
the curb of property lines not paid from the Sewer Construction Fund 
(as defined in the Sewer Bond 
Resolution), refunds of moneys lawfully due to others, and any other current expenses required or 
permitted to be paid by the Authority under the provisions of the Genera l Bond Resolution or by 
law, all to the extent properly and directly at tributable to the Sewer System under generally 
accepted accounting principles, expenses in connection with the authorization of the Sewer Bonds 
and the expenses, liabilities and compensation of any Fiduciary (as defined in the Sewer Bond 
Resolution) required to be paid under the General Bond Resolution, but not including any reserves 
for operation, maintenance or repa ir or any allowance for deprecia tion, amortization, interest on 
obligations of the Authority, including the First Lien Sewer Bonds and the Sewer Bonds, or similar 
charges. 
 
In the opinion of Bond Counsel to the Authority, the provisions of the Sewer Notes and the Sewer 
Note Resolution are deemed to be and do constitute contracts by and among the Authority, the 
Trustee and the holders, from time to time, of the Sewer Notes, and the pledge that is made in the 
Sewer Note Resolution and the covenants and agre ements that are set forth in the Sewer Note 
Resolution to be performed by or  on behalf of the Authority sh all be for the equal benefit, 
protection and security of the holders of any and all Sewer Notes and Additional Project Notes, all 
of which, regardless of the time or times of their issuance or maturity, shall be of equal rank without 
preference, priority or distinct ion of any of the Sewer Notes ove r any other thereof, except as 
expressly provided in or pursuant to the terms of the Sewer Note Resolution.  
 
The Authority has no power to levy or collect taxes.  The Sewer Notes are not a debt or a liability 
of the State, the City (except to the extent set forth in the Sewer Service Contract) or any other 
political subdivision of the State, other than the Authority.  Neither the members of the Authority, 
nor any person executing the Sewer Notes shall be  liable personally thereon by reason of the 
issuance thereof. 
 
Sewer Bonds 
 
The Authority is authorized to issue the Sewer Bonds pursuant to the Sewer Bond Resolution and 
has issued the Sewer Bonds as Additional Bonds  pursuant to Section 317 of the General Bond 
Resolution.  The Sewer Bonds are direct and special obligations of the Authority, and the full faith 
and credit of the Authority is pledged to the pa yment of the principal of  and the interest on the 
Sewer Bonds set forth in the Sewer Bond Reso lution.  The Sewer Bonds are payable from and  
20 
  
 
 
secured by the Sewer Revenues and from other moneys which are pledged therefor under the terms 
of the Sewer Bond Resolution, subject to the right of the Authority to provide for Sewer Operating 
Expenses, as hereinafter defined, and further subject to the prior pledge of Sewer Revenues to the 
First Lien Sewer Bonds, but also collaterally the Water Revenues (as defined under the subheading 
“Water Bonds” herein). However, the power a nd the obligation of the Authority to cause 
application of Water Revenues (if Sewer Revenues are insufficient) to the payment of the principal 
of or the interest on the Sewer Bonds are subject to: (1) the prior ri ghts of the holders of the First 
Lien Water Bonds of the City issued in accordance with and as defined and refe rred to in the 
General Bond Resolution and to the prior obligation of the Authority to provide from such Water 
Revenues or other funds amounts for the payment or the security of the principal of or the interest 
on such First Lien Water Bonds; and (2) the right of the Author ity to cause amounts to be 
withdrawn and paid on account fo r Water Operating Expenses of the Water System or for other 
purposes under the provisions of Section 510 of the General Bond Resolution.  There are no First 
Lien Sewer Bonds or First Lien Water Bonds  outstanding. See “Outstanding Sewer Bonds”, 
“Authority Water Bonds”, and “City Water Bonds” below for a description of the outstanding 
Sewer Bonds, Authority Water Bonds, and City Water Bonds, respectively. 
 
The term “Sewer Revenues” under the Sewer Bond Resolution means all fees, rents, charges, 
including Sewer Service Charges and Annual Sewer Charges (each as defined in the Sewer Bond 
Resolution) and all other income derived or to  be derived by the Aut hority from or for the 
operation, use or services of the Sewer System, excluding any Government Grant (as defined in 
the Sewer Bond Resolution).  
 
The term “Sewer Operating Expenses” under th e Sewer Bond Resolution means the Authority's 
reasonable and necessary current expenses of maintaining, repairing and operating the Sewer 
System, including without limita tion, all administrative, genera l and commercial expenses, 
insurance and surety bond premiums, payments to others for the collection, treatment or disposal 
of sewage and other wastes, engineering expenses, legal expenses, auditing expenses, payments to 
pension, retirement, health and hospitalization fu nds, any taxes that may be lawfully imposed on 
the Authority or its income or operations or the property under its control, ordinary and current 
rentals of equipment or other property, usual expenses of maintenance and repair, costs of service 
connections from mains to the curb of property lines not paid from the Sewer Construction Fund, 
refunds of moneys lawfully due to others, and any other current expenses required or permitted to 
be paid by the Authority under the provisions of the General Bond Resolution or by law, all to the 
extent properly and directly attributable to the Sewer System under generally accepted accounting 
principles, expenses in connection with the auth orization of the Sewer Bonds and the expenses, 
liabilities and compensation of any Fiduciary required to be paid hereunder, but not including any 
reserves for operation, maintenance or repair or any allowa nce for depreciati on, amortization, 
interest on obligations of the Authority, including th e First Lien Sewer Bonds and the Sewer 
Bonds, or similar charges.

are deemed to be and do consti tute contracts by a nd among the Authority, the Trustee and the

covenants and agreements that are set forth in the Resolution to be performed by or on behalf of

the Authority shall be for the equal benefit, prot ection and security of th e holders of any and all

(as defined in the General Water Note Resolution), fees, rents, charges and all other income derived

Authority's reasonable and necessary current expenses of maintaining, repairing and operating the 
Water System, including without limitation, all administrative, general and commercial expenses, 
insurance and surety bond premiums, engineeri ng expenses, legal expens es, auditing expenses,

and current rentals of equipment or other property, usual expenses of maintenance and repair, costs

Water Bonds 
 
The Authority is authorized to issue the Water Bonds and the Taxable Bonds (together, the “Water 
System Bonds”) pursuant to the Water Bond Reso lution and has issued the Water System Bonds 
as Additional Bonds pursuant to Section 317 of the General Bond Resolution.  The Water System 
Bonds are direct and special obligations of th e Authority, and the full faith and credit of the 
Authority is pledged to the payment of the principal of and the interest on the Water System Bonds 
as set forth in the Water Bond Resolution.  The Water Bonds are payable from and secured by the 
Water Revenues, as hereinafter defined, subject to the prior pledge of such Water Revenues to the 
payment of Water Operating Expenses, as herein after defined, of the Wat er System and further 
subject to the pledge of the First Lien Water Bo nds, but also collaterally the Sewer Revenues (as 
defined under the subheading “Sewer Bonds” herein ). However, the power and the obligation of 
the Authority to cause application of Sewer Reve nues (if Water Revenues are insufficient) to the 
payment of the principal of or the interest on the Water Bonds ar e subject to: (1) the prior rights 
of the holders of the First Lien Sewer Bonds of  the Authority issued in accordance with and as 
defined and referred to in the Sewer Bond Resolution and to the prior obligation of the Authority 
to provide from such Sewer Revenues or other f unds amounts for the paymen t or the security of 
the principal of or the interest on such First Lien  Sewer Bonds; and (2) the right of the Authority 
to cause amounts to be withdrawn and paid on account for Sewer Operating Expenses (as defined 
under the subheading “Sewer Bonds” herein) of the Sewer System or for other purposes under the 
provisions of Section 510 of the General Bond Resolution.  There are no First Lien Sewer Bonds 
or First Lien Water Bonds outstanding. See “O utstanding Sewer Bonds”, “Authority Water 
Bonds”, and “City Water Bonds” below for a description of the outstanding Sewer Bonds, 
Authority Water Bonds, and City Water Bonds, respectively.  
 
The term “Water Revenues” under the Water Bond Resolution means all fees, rents, charges 
including Water Service Charges and Annual Water  Charges (each as defined in the Water Bond 
Resolution) and all other income derived or to  be derived by the Aut hority from or for the 
operation, use or services of the Water System or derived or to be derived by the Authority from 
any other source and deposited into the Wate r Revenue Fund (as defined in the Water Bond 
Resolution); provided however, that “Water Revenues” shall not include any Governmental Grant.

The term “Water Operating Expenses” under th e Water Bond Resolution means the Authority's

engineering expenses, legal expenses, auditing expenses, payments to pension, retirement, health

Bonds and the Water Bonds, or similar charges.
