PAGE 1 OF 14 
THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
SUPPLEMENTAL RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE ISSUANCE OF 
NOT TO EXCEED $20,000,000 SEWER REVENUE BONDS, AND 
IN ANTICIPATION THEREOF, PROJECT NOTES, OF THE JERSEY 
CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
MOTIONED BY: COMMISSIONER BROWN 
SECONDED BY: COMMISSIONER HARTYE 
WHEREAS, on January 28, 1998, the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority (the 
“Authority”), a public body corporate and politic of the State of New Jersey organized pursuant to the Municipal and County Utilities Authorities Law (N.J.S.A. 40:14B-1 et sea.) (the “Act”) adopted a resolution entitled, “Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Revenue Bonds of the 
Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority”, as amended and supplemented (the “General Bond 
Resolution”); and 
WHEREAS, Section 317 of the General Bond Resolution provides for the issuance by the Authority of Additional Sewer Bonds for the purpose of raising funds for Additional Sewer 
Projects, each as defined in the General Bond Resolution, upon the terms and conditions set forth in the General Bond Resolution; and 
WHEREAS, on May 28, 1998, the Authority adopted a resolution entitled “Resolution 
Authorizing the Issuance of Project Notes of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority”, as amended on July 27, 2000, as further amended and supplemented (the “Sewer Project Note 
Resolution”); and 
WHEREAS, Section 311 of the Sewer Project Note Resolution provides for the issuance by the Authority of Additional Project Notes (as defined in the Sewer Project Note Resolution) for 
the purpose of raising funds for the Cost of acquisition or construction of part of parts of the System 
(as defined in the Sewer Project Note Resolution) (each, an “Additional Sewer Project”), upon the terms and conditions set forth in the Sewer Project Note Resolution; and 
WHEREAS, the Authority desires to undertake the various projects described more particularly on Exhibit A, attached hereto (each, a “Project” and collectively, the “Projects”); and 
WHEREAS, in accordance with the provisions of Section 317 of the General Bond 
Resolution, the Authority intends to hereby authorize one or more series of Additional Sewer Bonds of the Authority designated as “Sewer Revenue Bonds, Series 20__” (with such series 
designation to reflect the calendar year of issuance of each such series of bonds) (collectively, the “Bonds”), in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed $20,000,000, to provide for the long- 
term financing of the Projects; and 
3/2580 
PAGE 3 OF 14 
THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
RESOLUTION 
“City” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Code” shall be as defined in Section 503. 
“Cost” shall have the meaning given to such term in the Act. 
“General Bond Resolution” shall have the meaning given to such term in therecitals hereof. 
“Note Certificate” shall be as defined in Section 404(A). 
“Obligations” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Official Statement” shall be as defined in Section 502(A). 
“Preliminary Official Statement” shall be as defined in Section 502(A). 
“Project” or “Projects” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Project Notes” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Purchase Agreement” shall be as defined in Section 304(E). 
“Rule” shall be as defined in Section 501(A). 
“State” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Trustee” means Manufacturers and Traders Trust Company, Wilmington, Delaware, 
successor in interest to Capital One Bank, Jersey City, New Jersey, successor in interest to North 
Fork Bank, Jersey City, New Jersey, successor in interest to The Trust Company, Jersey City, New 
Jersey. 
“Underwriter” shall be as defined in Section 304(A). 
“Sewer Project Note Resolution” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals 
hereof. 
Section 103. Incorporation of General Bond Resolution, This 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution supplements and amends the General Bond Resolution where applicable. With 
respect to the provisions hereof applicable to the Bonds, the General Bond Resolution is incorporated herein by reference thereto. 
3/2530 
PAGE 2 OF 14 
THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
RESOLUTION 
WHEREAS, in anticipation of the issuance of the Bonds, and in accordance with the 
provisions of Section 311 of the Sewer Project Note Resolution, the Authority intends to hereby authorize one or more series of Additional Project Notes of the Authority designated as “Project 
Notes, Series 20__” (with such series designation to reflect the calendar year of issuance of such series of project notes) (collectively, the “Project Notes” and, together with the Bonds, the 
“Obligations”), in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed $20,000,000, to provide for the 
short-term financing of the Projects; and 
WHEREAS, on June 14, 2017 and October 24, 2018, the City of Jersey City, New Jersey 
(the “City”), respectively adopted Resolutions 17-501 and 18-940, consenting to the undertaking 
by the Authority of the Projects, as required by the Service Contract between the Authority and the City dated as of December 1, 1985, as amended and supplemented; and 
WHEREAS, the Authority desires to specify and determine certain terms and conditions 
of the Obligations being issued; 
NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED BY THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL 
UTILITIES AUTHORITY, and the members and commissioners thereof as follows: 
ARTICLEIL 
Section 101. Short Title, This resolution may hereinafter be cited by the Authority and 
is hereinafter sometimes referred to as the “2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution”. 
Section 102. Definitions, Terms which are used as defined terms herein shall, unless 
specifically defined herein or unless the context clearly requires otherwise, have the meanings assigned to such terms in the General Bond Resolution. 
“Act” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Additional Sewer Project” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Authority” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Authorized Officer” shall be as defined in Section 302. 
“Bond Insurer” shall be as defined in Section 304(F). 
“Bonds” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Certificate” shall be as defined in Section 304(A). 
3/2530 
PAGE 4 OF 14 
THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
RESOLUTION 
Section 104. Incorporation of Sewer Project Note Resolution, This 2025 
Supplemental Sewer Resolution supplements and amends the Sewer Project Note Resolution where applicable. With respect to the provisions hereof applicable to the Project Notes, the Sewer 
Project Note Resolution is incorporated herein by reference thereto. 
Section 105. Severability of Invalid Provisions, If any one or more of the covenants or agreements provided in this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, on the part of the Authority or 
the Trustee, to be performed should be contrary to law, then such covenant or covenants, agreement or agreements, shall be deemed separable from the remaining covenants and agreements and shall 
in no way affect the validity of the other provisions of the 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution or of any Obligation. 
ARTICLE IL 
Section 201. Authority for 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, This 2025 
Supplemental Sewer Resolution is adopted pursuant to the Act and the General Bond Resolution and Sewer Project Note Resolution, as applicable, and the Authority has ascertained and hereby determines that each and every act, matter, thing or course of conduct as to which provision is 
made in this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, is appropriate in order to carry out and effectuate the purposes of the Authority in accordance with the Act and the General Bond 
Resolution and Sewer Project Note Resolution, as applicable, to further secure the payment of the principal of and interest on the Obligations. 
Section 202. Bonds to Constitute Additional Sewer Bonds. The Bonds shall constitute 
Additional Sewer Bonds as such termis defined in the General Bond Resolution and shall be issued 
pursuant to and in accordance with Section 317? of the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 203. Project Notes to Constitute Additional Project Notes, The Project Notes 
shall constitute Additional Project Notes as such term is defined in the Sewer Project Note 
Resolution and shall be issued pursuant to and in accordance with Section 311 of the Sewer Project 
Note Resolution. 
Section 204. 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution to Constitute Contract Amongst 
Purchasers of Bonds, the Authority and the Trustee, In consideration of the purchase and 
acceptance of the Bonds, the provisions of this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution applicable 
to the Bonds shall be deemed to be and shall constitute a contract between the purchasers of such Bonds, the Authority, and the Trustee; the pledge made in this 2025 Supplemental Sewer 
Resolution and the covenants and agreements herein set forth to be performed by or on behalf of the Authority shall be for the equal benefit, protection and security of the holders of the Bonds, all 
of which, regardless of the time or times of their issue or maturity, shall be of equal rank without preference, priority or distinction of any of the Bonds over any other thereof except as expressly 
provided in or pursuant to the General Bond Resolution.3/25/30 
PAGES OF 14 
THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
RESOLUTION 
Section 205. 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution to Constitute Contract Amongst 
Purchasers of Project Notes, the Authority and the Trustee. In consideration of the purchase 
and acceptance of the Project Notes, the provisions of this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution applicable to the Project Notes shall be deemed to be and shall constitute a contract between the 
purchasers of such Project Notes, the Authority, and the Trustee; the pledge made in this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution and the covenants and agreements herein set forth to be performed 
by or on behalf of the Authority shall be forthe equal benefit, protection and security of the holders 
of the Project Notes, all of which, regardless of the time or times of their issue or maturity, shall be of equal rank without preference, priority or distinction of any of the Project Notes over any other thereof except as expressly provided in or pursuant to the Sewer Project Note Resolution. 
Section 206. Estimated Cost of the Projects. The Authority hereby determines that the aggregate estimated Cost of the Projects shall not exceed $20,000,000, inclusive of any original 
issue discount (but not original issue premium), capitalized interest and all reserves, and exclusive 
of accrued interest on any Obligations. 
ARTICLE IIL 
Section 301. Authorization and Purpose of the Bonds, The Bonds of the Authority in 
the principal amount of not to exceed $20,000,000 are hereby authorized to be issued in one or mare series for the purpose of paying a portion of the Cost of the Projects. 
Section 302. Sale and Terms of Bonds, The Executive Director, Chairperson and Vice 
Chairperson of the Authority (each, an “Authorized Officer”) are hereby designated as the individuals who shall have the power to determine, among other things (a) the principal amount 
of each series of Bonds to be issued, in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed $20,000,000, (b) the time, date, series, denomination(s), maturity dates, redemption provisions, manner of sale 
(including which Bonds are sold through the New Jersey Water Bank) and place of payment of 
each series of Bonds, (c) the rate of interest for each series of Bonds, and (d) such other tems and conditions as may be necessary or related to the sale of each series of the Bonds. The Authorized Officers are hereby designated as thie individuals who shall have the power to sell each series of 
the Bonds, subject to the conditions and limitations set forth herein. 
Section 303. Reserved. 
Section 304, Bonds. (A) Each series of Bonds may be sold at public or private sale as 
determined by an Authorized Officer. If sold at private sale, such series of Bonds may be sold to an underwriter or other members of the banking community as may be appointed by an Authorized 
Officer (collectively, the “Underwriter”). Such Authorized Officers are each hereby authorized to execute a Purchase Agreement or other similar document, if any, in connection with the sale of 
each series of Bonds and award each series of Bonds to the Underwriter, with such award being 
3/2530 
PAGE7 OF 14 
THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
RESOLUTION 
delivery of each series of Bonds, shall determine the terms and conditions relating to the sale of each series of Bonds, including the rate of interest to be borne by each series of Bonds and the 
underwriter’s discount, if any, which is payable to the Underwriter in connection with the sale of each series of Bonds. Each series of Bonds shall be delivered to the Underwriter at such time and 
place as shall be determined by the Authority, subject to the terms and conditions of the Purchase Agreement. The Authorized Officers, the Secretary and the Assistant Secretary of the Authority 
are, and each of them is, hereby authorized and directed to do and perform all things and execute 
all papers in the name of the Authority, and to make all payments necessary or in their opinion convenient, to the end that the Authority may carry out its obligations under the terms of said 
Purchase Agreement. 
(F) — Ifnecessary, an Authorized Officer shall arrange for any necessary bond insurance in order to obtain the best possible interest rates and the most cost effective financing in accordance 
with the recommendation of the Underwriter. To the extent that bond insurance is necessary, the bond insurer (the “Bond Insurer”) shall be deemed to be the sole holder of the applicable series of 
Bonds for the purpose of exercising any voting right or privilege or giving any consent or direction or taking any other action that the holders of the applicable series of Bonds are entitled to take 
pursuant to the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 305, No Recourse on the Bonds. No recourse shall be had for the payment of the principal of or the interest on the Bonds or for any claim based thereon or on the 2025 
Supplemental Sewer Resolution or the General Bond Resolution against any member or other officer of the Authority or any person executing the Bonds. The Bonds are not and shall not be in 
any way a debt or liability of the State of New Jersey or of any county or municipality and do not and shall not create or constitute any indebtedness, liability or obligation of said State or of any 
county or municipality, either legal, moral or otherwise. 
Section 306. Execution of Bonds. The Chairperson or Vice Chairperson of the Authority is hereby authorized to execute by the manual or facsimile signature each series of Bonds 
in the name and on behalf of the Authority attested by the manual or facsimile signature of its Secretary or Assistant Secretary, and following such execution and attestation, such bonds shall 
be delivered to the Trustee for authentication, and the Trustee is hereby authorized to authenticate such Bonds upon the fulfillment of the conditions set forth in Section 318 of the General Bond 
Resolution. 
Section 307. Appointment of Trustee, In accordance with the provisions of the General Bond Resolution, as amended, the Trustee is hereby appointed to serve as trustee of each series of 
Bonds and shall accept the carry out its obligations as such, including but not limited to, 
authentication of each series of Bonds. 
3/2530 
PAGE 6 OF 14 
THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
RESOLUTION 
evidenced by the execution of a certificate by an Authorized Officer (the “Certificate”). The 
Purchase Agreement, if any, and the Certificate shall determine the terms and conditions relating to the sale of each series of Bonds, including the rate of interest to be borne by such series of Bonds 
and the Underwriter’ discount, if any, which is payable to the underwriter in connection with the sale of such series of Bonds; provided however, that without the further authorization of the 
Authority, the Underwriter’s total compensation for the sale of any such series of Bonds shall not exceed $6.00 per $1,000 principal amount of such series of Bonds, it being understood that the 
structure of any such financing may include an Underwriter’s discount, original issue discount or premium above the par amount of such series of Bonds. The Purchase Agreement, if any, and the 
Certificate shall contain such other terms and conditions as shall be deemed to be necessary in connection with the sale of suchseries of Bonds. The Certificate shall be presented to the members 
of the Authority at the next regular meeting following such sale and award as evidence of the terms 
and details of the sale of the applicable series of Bonds.

such series designation to reflect the calendar year of issuance of each series of the Bonds). Each

Resolution.

Sewer Resolution.

evidenced by the execution of each such certificate or other document by the party authorized hereunder to execute such certificate or other document, and (ii) perform such other actions as the 
Authorized Officers deem necessary, desirable or convenient in relation to the execution thereof.

ARTICLE IV 
Section 401. Authorization and Purpose of the Project Notes, The Project Notes of 
the Authority in the principal amount of not to exceed $20,000,000 are hereby authorized to be 
issued in one or more series, and in anticipation of the issuance of Bonds, for the purpose of paying a portion of the Cost of the Projects. 
Section 402. Sale and Terms of Project Notes. The Authorized Officers are hereby designated as the individuals who shall have the power to determine, among other things (a) the principal amount of each series of Project Notes to be issued, in an aggregate principal amount not 
to exceed $20,000,000, (b) the time, date, series, denomination(s), maturity dates, redemption provisions, manner of sale (including which Project Notes are sold through the New Jersey Water 
Bank) and place of payment of each series of Project Notes, (c) the rate of interest for each series 
of Project Notes, and (d) such other terms and conditions as may be necessary orrelatedto the sale of each series of the Project Notes. The Authorized Officers are hereby designated as the 
individuals who shall have the power to sell each series of the Project Notes, subject to the conditions and limitations set forth herein. 
Section 403. Reserved. 
Section 404. Project Notes. (A) Each series of Project Notes may be sold at public or private sale as determined by an Authorized Officer. If sold at private sale, such series of Project Notes may be sold to an Underwriter. Such Authorized Officers are each hereby authorized to 
execute a Purchase Agreement or other similar document, if any, in connection with the sale of each series of Project Notes and award each series of Project Notes to the Underwriter, with such 
award being evidenced by the execution of a certificate by an Authorized Officer (the “Note 
Certificate”). The Purchase Agreement, if any, and the Note Certificate shall determine the terms and conditions relating to the sale of each series of Project Notes, including the rate of interest to 
be bome by such series of Project Notes and the Underwriter’s discount, if any, which is payable to the underwriter in connection with the sale of such series of Project Notes. The Purchase Agreement, if any, and the Note Certificate shall contain such other terms and conditions as shall 
be deemed to be necessary in connection with the sale of such series of Project Notes. The Note Certificate shall be presented to the members of the Authority at the next regular meeting following 
such sale and award as evidence of the terms and details of the sale of the applicable series of Project Notes. 
(B) Bach series of Project Notes shall be entitled “Sewer Project Notes, Series 20_” (with such series designation to reflect the calendar year of issuance of each series of the Project 
Notes). Each series of Project Notes shall be in substantially the form described and contained in the Sewer Project Note Resolution.3/25/30

(C)_ Allof the proceeds of sale of any Project Notes, including accrued interest (ifany) received upon delivery thereof, shall, simultaneously with the issuance of such series of Project 
Notes, be paid and applied by the Authority in accordance with the Sewer Project Note Resolution and this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution and as provided in an Order of the Authority 
executed by an Authorized Officer consistent with the Sewer Project Note Resolution and this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution.

deliver, and the Secretary or Assistant Secretary of the Authority is hereby further authorized to 
attest to such execution and to affix the corporate seal of the Authority to, any document, instrument or closing certificate deemed necessary, desirable or convenient by the Authorized

execution and delivery of each series of Project Notes and the consummation of the transaction contemplated thereby, which determination shall be conclusively evidenced by the execution of 
each such certificate or other document by the party authorized hereunder to execute such certificate or other document, and (ii) perform such other actions as the Authorized Officers deem 
necessary, desirable or convenient in relation to the execution thereof. 
(E) I fnecessary, an Authorized Officer shall arrange for any necessary bond insurance inorder to obtain the best possible interest rates and the most cost effective financing in accordance 
with the recommendation of the Underwriter and the Authority’s municipal advisor. To the extent 
that bond insurance is necessary, the Bond Insurer shall be deemed to be the sole holder of the applicable series of Project Notes for the purpose of exercising any voting right or privilege or giving any consent or direction or taking any other action that the holders of the applicable series 
of Project Notes are entitled to take pursuant to the Sewer Project Note Resolution. 
Section 405. No Recourse on Project Notes. No recourse shall be had for the payment 
of the principal of or the interest on the Project Notes or for any claim based thereon or on the 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution or the Sewer Project Note Resolution against any member 
or other officer of the Authority or any person executing the Project Notes. The Project Notes are not and shall not be in any way a debt or liability of the State of New Jersey or of any county or 
municipality and do not and shall not create or constitute any indebtedness, liability or obligation of said State or of any county or municipality, either legal, moral or otherwise. 
Section 406, Execution of Project Notes, The Chairperson or Vice Chairperson of the 
Authority is hereby authorized to execute by the manual or facsimile signature each series of Project Notes in the name and on behalf of the Authority attested by the manual or facsimile 
signature of its Secretary or Assistant Secretary, and following such execution and attestation, such Project Notes shall be delivered to the Trustee for authentication, and the Trustee is hereby 
authorized to authenticate such Project Notes upon the fulfillment of the conditions set forth in Section 312 of the Sewer Project Note Resolution.

Authority hereby authorizes each such Preliminary Official Statement and the information 
contained therein to be used in connection with the offering and sale of the respective series of Obligations and authorizes the Underwriter to distribute such Preliminary Official Statement, in 
electronic or hard copy form, to prospective purchasers of the applicable series of Obligations.

of the applicable Purchase Contract and to be substantially in the form of the Preliminary Official 
Statement with such changes therein as shall be approved by an Authorized Officer, in consultation with Bond Counsel, and by the Underwriter. The Authority hereby authorizes the execution of each Official Statement by such Authorized Officer, the delivery thereof to the applicable 
Underwriter and the distribution of the Official Statement in connection with the offering and sale 
of the applicable Obligations. 
Section 503. Covenant of Authority as to Compliance with Federal Tax Matters. 
The Authority hereby covenants that it will take all actions within its control that are necessary to 
assure that interest on any tax-exempt Obligations is excludable from gross income under the 
Intemal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended (the “Code”), and the Authority will refrain from taking any action that would adversely affect the exclusion of interest on any tax-exempt 
Obligations from gross income under the provisions of the Code. 
Section $04. Declaration of Intent. (A) The Authority reasonably expects to reimburse 
its expenditure of Costs of the Projects paid prior to the issuance of the Obligations with proceeds of its Obligations. This resolution is intended to be and hereby is a declaration of the Authority's 
official intent to reimburse the expenditure of Costs of the Projects paid prior to the issuance of the Obligations with the proceeds of one or more borrowings to be incurred by the Authority, in 
accordance with Treasury Regulations §150-2. The aggregate maximum principal amount of the 
Obligations expected to be issued to finance the Projects is not to exceed $20,000,000. The Costs of the Projects to be reimbursed with the proceeds of the Obligations will be "capital expenditures” 
in accordance with the meaning of Section 150 of the Code. 
(B) No reimbursement allocation will employ an “abusive arbitrage device” under 
Treasury Regulations § 1.148-10 to avoid the arbitrage restrictions or to avoid the restrictions under 
Sections 142 through 147, inclusive, of the Code. The proceeds of the Obligations used to reimburse the Authority for Costs of the Projects, or funds corresponding to such amounts, will 
not be used in a manner that results in the creation of "replacement proceeds”, including "sinking funds’, "pledged funds” or funds subject to a "negative pledge” (as such terms are defined in Treasury Regulations §1.148-1), of the Obligations or another issue of debt obligations of the 
Authority, other than amounts deposited into a "bona fide debt service fund’ (as defined in 
Treasury Regulations §1.148-1).

ARTICLE V 
Section $01. Continuing Market Disclosure. (A) Solely for purposes of complying with Rule 15c2-12 of the Securities and Exchange Commission, as amended and interpreted from time to time (the “Rule”), provided that (i) the Obligations are not exempt from the Rule, (ii) the 
I-Bank has determined that the Obligations are not exempt from the following requirements and/or 
(iii) the Obligations are not exempt from the following requirements in accordance with paragraph (A) of the Rule, for so long as the Obligations remain outstanding (unless the Obligations have 
been wholly defeased), the Authority shall provide for the benefit of the holders of the Obligations and the beneficial owners thereof all information required under the Rule. 
(B) If all or any part of the Rule ceases to be in effect for any reason, then the 
information required to be provided under this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, insofar as the provision of the Rule no longer in effect required the provision of such information, shall no 
longer be required to be provided. 
(©) The Executive Director is hereby authorized to enter into additional written contracts or undertakings to implement the Rule and is further authorized to amend such contracts 
or undertakings or the undertakings set forth in this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, provided such amendment is, in the opinion of nationally recognized bond counsel, in compliance 
with the Rule or would have been in compliance with the Rule if such amended undertaking had 
been entered into at the time of the issuance of the applicable series of Obligations. 
(D) Inthe event that the Authority fails to comply with the Rule or the written contracts or undertakings specified in this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, the Authority shall not be liable for monetary damages, remedy being hereby specifically limited to specific performance of 
the Rule requirements or the written contracts or undertakings therefor. 
Section $02. Distribution of Preliminary Official Statement; Approval of Official Statement. (A) The Authority hereby authorizes the preparation and distribution of a preliminary 
official statement (each a “Preliminary Official Statement”) relating to each series of Obligations, 
substantially in such form as shall be approved by an Authorized Officer in consultation with Bond Counsel. As of the date of each such Preliminary Official Statement, such Authorized Officer, in consultation with Bond Counsel, shall make the determination that the Authority deems such 
Preliminary Official Statement “final”, as that term is used in paragraph (b)(1) of the Rule, except for the omission of no more than the information permitted by paragraph (b)(L) of the Rule. The

JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
SUPPLEMENTAL BOND RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE 
ISSUANCE OF NOT TO EXCEED $80,000,000 SEWER REVENUE 
BONDS, SERIES 2025 OF THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL 
UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
MOTIONED BY: COMMISSIONER BROWN 
SECONDED BY; COMMISSIONER HARTYE 
WHEREAS, on January 28, 1998, the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority (the 
“Authority”), a public body corporate and politic of the State of New Jersey organized pursuant to the Municipal and County Utilities Authorities Law (N.JS.A. 40:14B-1 et. sea) (the “Act”) 
adopted a resolution entitled, “Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Revenue Bonds of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority”, as amended and supplemented (the “General Bond 
Resolution”); and 
WHEREAS, Scction 317 of the General Bond Resolution provides for the issuance by the Authority of Additional Sewer Bonds for the purpose of raising funds for Additional Sewer 
Projects, each as defined in the General Bond Resolution, upon the terms and conditions set forth in the General Bond Resolution; and 
WHEREAS, the Authority desires to undertake the Additional Sewer Project described 
more particularly on Exhibit A, attached hereto (the “2025 Project”); and 
WHEREAS, in accordance with the provisions of Section 317 of the General Bond Resolution, the Authority intends to hereby authorize a series of Additional Sewer Bonds of the 
Authority designated as “Sewer Revenue Bonds, Series 2025” (the “2025 Bonds”) in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed $80,000,000, to provide for the financing of the 2025 Project; and 
WHEREAS, the Authority desires to specify and determine certain terms and conditions 
of the 2025 Bonds being issued; 
NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED BY THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL 
UTILITIES AUTHORITY, and the members and commissioners thereof as follows: 
3/25/29 
PAGE 3 OF 10 
JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
RESOLUTION 
Section 104, Severability of Invalid Provisions. Ifany one or more of the covenants or 
agreements provided in this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, on the part of the Authority or the Trustee, to be performed should be contrary to law, then such covenant or covenants, agreement or agreements, shall be deemed separable from the remaining covenants and agreements and shall 
inno way affect the validity of the other provisions of the 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution or of any 2025 Bond. 
ARTICLE I 
Section 201. Authority for 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, This 2025 
Supplemental Sewer Resolution is adopted pursuant to the Act and the General Bond Resolution 
and the Authority has ascertained and hereby determines that each and every act, matter, thing or course of conduct as to which provision is made in this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, is 
appropriate in order to carry out and effectuate the purposes of the Authority in accordance with the Act and the General Bond Resolution to further secure the payment of the principal of and 
interest on the 2025 Bonds. 
Section 202. 2025 Bonds to Constitute Additional Sewer Bonds. The 2025 Bonds shall 
constitute Additional Sewer Bonds as such term is defined in the General Bond Resolution and 
shall be issued pursuant to and in accordance with Section 317 of the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 203. 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution to Constitute Contract, In 
consideration of the purchase and acceptance of the 2025 Bonds, the provisions of this 2025 
Supplemental Sewer Resolution shall be deemed to be and shall constitute a contact between the purchasers of such bonds, the Authority, and the Trustee; the pledge made in this 2025 
Supplemental Sewer Resolution and the covenants and agreements herein set forth to be performed by or on behalfof the Authority shall be for the equal benefit, protection and security of the holders 
of the 2025 Bonds, all of which, regardless of the time or times of their issue or maturity, shall be of equal rank without preference, priority or distinction of any of the 2025 Bonds over any other 
thereof except as expressly provided in or pursuant to the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 204. Estimated Cost of 2025 Project. The Authority hereby determines that the aggregate estimated Cost (as defined in the Act) of the 2025 Project shal! not exceed $80,000,000, 
inclusive of any original issue discount (but not original issue premium), capitalized interest and all reserves, and exclusive of accrued interest on the 2025 Bonds.

Section 101. Short Title, This resolution may hereinafter be cited by the Authority and 
is hereinafter sometimes referred to as the “2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution”.

specifically defined herein or unless the context clearly requires otherwise, have the meanings 
assigned to such terms in the General Bond Resolution. 
“2025 Bonds” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“2025 Project” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Act” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Authority” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Bond Insurer” shall be as defined in Section 308. 
“Certificate” shall be as defined in Section 302(A). 
“Code” shall be as defined in Section 311. 
“General Bond Resolution” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Official Statement” shall be as defined in Section 310(B). 
“Preliminary Official Statement” shall be as defined in Section 310(A). 
“Purchase Agreement” shall be as defined in Section 307, 
“Rule” shall be as defined in Section 309. 
“Trustee” means M&T Bank, Jersey City, New Jersey, as set forth in Section 306 of this 
2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution. 
“Underwriter” shall be as defined in Section 302¢A). 
Section 103. Incorporation of General Bond Resolution, This 2025 Supplemental 
Sewer Resolution supplements and amends the General Bond Resolution. The General Bond 
Resolution is incorporated herein by reference thereto.

ARTICLE III 
Section 301. Authorization and Purpose of the 2025 Bonds, The 2025 Bonds of the 
Authority in the principal amount of not to exceed $80,000,000 are hereby authorized to be issued for the purpose of paying a portion of the Cost of the 2025 Project. Such 2025 Bonds shall be 
entitled “Sewer Revenue Bonds, Series 2025”. 
Section 302. Description and Terms of 2025 Bonds. 
(A) Sale and Terms of 2025 Bonds, The Executive Director or the Chairperson of the 
Authority are each hereby designated as the individuals who shall have the power to determine, 
among other things (a) the principal amount of 2025 Bonds to be issued, in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed $80,000,000, (b) the time, date, series, denomination(s), maturity dates, 
redemption provisions, manner of sale and place of payment of the 2025 Bonds, (c) the rate of interest for such 2025 Bonds, provided such average rate shall not exceed 7.00%, and (d) such other terms and conditions as may be necessary or related to the sale of the 2025 Bonds. The 
Executive Director or the Chairperson of the Authority are hereby designated as the individuals who shall have the power to sell the 2025 Bonds, subject to the following limitations. The 2025 
Bonds of the Authority may be sold to an underwriter or other members of the banking community as may be appointed by an Authority Officer (collectively, the “Underwriter”). The Authority 
Officers are each hereby authorized to execute a Purchase Agreement or other similar document, if any, in connection with the sale of the 2025 Bonds and award the 2025 Bonds to the Underwriter, 
with such award being evidenced by the execution of a certificate by the Executive Director or the Chairperson (the “Certificate”). The Purchase Agreement, if any, and the Certificate shall 
determine the terms and conditions relating to the sale of the 2025 Bonds, including the rate of 
interest to be borne by the 2025 Bonds and the Underwriter’s discount, if any, which is payable to the underwriter in connection with the sale of the 2025 Bonds; provided however, that without the further authorization of the Authority, the Underwriter's total compensation for the public offering 
of the 2025 Bonds shall not exceed $6.00 per $1,000 principal amount of such 2025 Bonds, it 
being understood that the structure of this financing may include an Underwriter’s discount, original issue discount or premium above the par amount of the 2025 Bonds. The Purchase 
Agreement, if any, and the Certificate shall contain such other terms and conditions as shall be deemed to be necessary in connection with the sale of the 2025 Bonds. The Certificate shall be presented to the members of the Authority at the next regular meeting following such sale and 
award as evidence of the terms and details of the sale of the 2025 Bonds. 
(B) Form of 2025 Bonds, The 2025 Bonds shall be in substantially the form described 
and contained in the General Bond Resolution. 
(C)  Eurther Authorizations, The Authority Officers are hereby further severally 
authorized to (i) execute and deliver, and the Secretary or Assistant Secretary of the Authority is hereby further authorized to attest to such execution and to affix the corporate seal of the Authority 
to, any document, instrument or closing certificate deemed necessary, desirable or convenient by3/25/29

RESOLUTION 
the Authority Officers, the Secretary or Assistant Secretary of the Authority, as applicable, in their 
respective sole discretion, after consultation with the Consultants, to be executed in connection with the execution and delivery of the 2025 Bonds and the consummation of the transaction contemplated thereby, which determination shall be conclusively evidenced by the execution of 
each such certificate or other document by the party authorized hereunder to execute such certificate or other document, and (ii) perform such other actions as the Authority Officers deem 
necessary, desirable or convenient in relation to the execution thereof. 
Section 303. Issuance of the 2025 Bonds and Application of Proceeds of Sale. The 
2025 Bonds authorized by Section 301 herein, are hereby directed to be executed by or on behalf of the Authority by its Authority Officer. All of the proceeds of sale of the 2025 Bonds, including 
accrued interest (if any) received upon delivery thereof, shall, simultaneously with the issuance of the 2025 Bonds, be paid and applied by the Authority in accordance with the General Bond 
Resolution and this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution and as provided in an Order of the 
Authority executed by the Executive Director or the Chairperson of the Authority consistent with the General Bond Resolution and this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution. 
Section 304. No Recourse on the 2025 Bonds. No recourse stiall be had for the payment of the principal of or the interest on the 2025 Bonds or for any claim based thereon or on the 2025 
Supplemental Sewer Resolution or the General Bond Resolution against any member or other officer of the Authority or any person exeéuting the 2025 Bonds. The 2025 Bonds are not and 
shall not be in any way a debt or liability of the State of New Jersey or of any county or municipality and do not and shall not create or constitute any indebtedness, liability or obligation 
of said State or of any county or municipality, either legal, moral or otherwise. 
Section 305. Execution of 2025 Bonds. The Chairperson or Vice Chairperson of the 
Authority is hereby authorized to execute by the manual or facsimile signature the 2025 Bonds in the name and on behalf of the Authority attested by the manual or facsimile signature of its 
Secretary or Assistant Secretary, and following such execution and attestation, such bonds shall be delivered to the Trustee for authentication, and the Trustee is hereby authorized to authenticate 
such Bonds upon the fulfillment of the applicable conditions set forth in Section 318 of the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 306. Appointment of Trustee, In accordance with the provisions of the General 
Bond Resolution, as amended, the Trustee is hereby appointed to serve as trustee of the 2025 Bonds and shall accept the carry out its obligations as such, including but not limited to, authentication of the 2025 Bonds.

Executive Director and Secretary of the Authority are, and each of them is hereby, authorized and directed to negotiate, execute and deliver a purchase agreement (the "Purchase Agreement") with the Underwriter. Executive Director is authorized to pay, or caused to be paid, Authority professional fees incurred 
in connection with the authorization, sale and issuance of the 2025 Bonds upon presentation of appropriate invoices setting forth the amount due. 
Section 313. Prior Action. All action which has been taken prior to the date hereof by 
the officers, employees, and agents of the Authority with respect to the sale of the 2025 Bonds, is hereby approved, ratified, adopted and confirmed. 
Section 314. Additional Acts, The Chairperson and any other officer of the Authority, 
and the Executive Director, staff and consultants of the Authority are hereby authorized and 
directed to effectuate the terms of this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution in connection with 
the issuance, sale and delivery of the 2025 Bonds.

RESOLUTION 
and conditions relating to the sale of the 2025 Bonds, including the rate of interest to be borne by the 2025 Bonds and the underwriter’s discount, if any, which is payable to the Underwriter in 
connection with the sale of the 2025 Bonds. The 2025 Bonds shall be delivered to the Underwriter at such time and place as shall be determined by the Authority, subject to the terms and conditions 
of the Purchase Agreement. The Chairperson, Vice Chairperson, Executive Director and Secretary 
of the Authority are, and each of them is, hereby authorized and directed to do and perform all things and execute all papers in the name of the Authority, and to make all payments necessary or in their opinion convenient, to the end that the Authority may carry out its obligations under the 
terms of said Purchase Agreement. 
Section 308. Payments Under Bond Insurance Policy, If necessary, the Executive 
Director shall arrange for any necessary bond insurance in order to obtain the best possible interest 
rates and the most cost effective financing in accordance with the recommendation of the Underwriter and/ or the Authority's municipal advisor. To the extent that bond insurance is necessary, the bond insurer (the “Bond Insurer”) shal! be deemed to be the sole holder of the 2025 
Bonds for the purpose of exercising any voting right or privilege or giving any consentor direction or taking any other action that the holders of the 2025 Bonds are entitled to take pursuant to the 
General Bond Resolution. 
Section 309. Continuing Market Disclosure. Solely for purposes of complying with Rule 15c2-12 of the Securities and Exchange Commission, as amended and interpreted from time 
to time (the "Rule"), and provided that the 2025 Bonds are not exempt from the Rule and provided that the 2025 Bonds are not exempt from the following requirements in accordance with paragraph 
(@) of the Rule, for so long as the 2025 Bonds remain outstanding (unless the 2025 Bonds have 
been wholly defeased), the Authority shall provide for the benefit of the holders of the 2025 Bonds and the beneficial owners thereof all information required under the Rule. If all or any part of the 
Rule ceases to be in effect for any reason, then the information required to be provided under this 
2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, insofar as the provision of the Rule no longer in effect required the provision of such information, shall no longer be required to be provided. The 
Executive Director is hereby authorized to enter into additional written contracts or undertakings to implement the Rule and is further authorized to amend such contracts or undertakings or the undertak ings set forth in this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, provided such amendment is, 
in the opinion of nationally recognized bond counsel, in compliance with the Rule or would have been in compliance with the Rule if such amended undertaking had been entered into at the time 
of the issuance of the 2025 Bonds. In the event that the Authority fails to comply with the Rule or the written contracts or undertakings specified in this 2025 Supplemental Sewer Resolution, the 
Authority shall not be liable for monetary damages, remedy being hereby specifically limited to specific performance of the Rule requirements or the written contracts or undertakings therefor.

covenanted in the Water System Service Contract that it will not, during the term of the Water 
System Service Contract, pledge water revenues to the holders of any Bonds or other obligations 
issued by the City after the adoption of this Resolution.

Section 204. General Bond Resolution to Constitute Contract. In consideration of the 
purchase and the acceptance of any or all of the Bonds by those who shall hold them from time to 
time, the provisions of the General Bond Resolution shall constitute a contract of the Authority with 
the holders from time to time of the Bonds. Except as otherwise stated herein, any pledge made in 
the General Bond Resolution and the provisions, the covenants and the agreements herein set forth 
to be performed by or on behalf of the Authority shall be for the equal benefit, protection and 
security of the holders of any and all of the Bonds. All of the Bonds. regardless of the time or the 
times oftheirissuance, shall be of equal rank without preference, priority or distinction of any of the 
Bonds over any other thereof except as expressly provided in or pursuant to the General Bond 
Resolution.

AUTHORIZATION, TERMS, EXECUTION AND

Section 30 I. Authorization of Bonds. (a) In accordance with the provisions of the Act and 
subject to and pursuant to the provisions of the General Bond Resolution and for the purpose of 
raising funds to pay the cost of a Sewer Project. Sewer Bonds of the Authority are hereby authorized 
to be issued by the Authority to pay for such cost as determined by resolution of the Authority. 
Sewer Bonds shall be issued from time to time and with such terms provided by resolutions of the 
Authority adopted from time to time to supplement and to implement this General Bond Resolution 
as hereinafter provided. 
(bl In accordance with the provisions of the Act and subject lo and pursuant to the 
provisions of the General Bond Resolution and for the purpose ofraising funds to par the cost of the 
Water Project. Water Bonds of the Authority are hereby authorized to be issued by the Authority to 
pay for the cost of the Water Project in an amount not to exceed $1 75,000,000 for the acquisition 
of the City's water francbise plus all related costs. The Authority expects to issue only $36,500,000 
Bonds for such purpose with the remainder of the deferred payments to the City being paid from 
Water Revenues and Sewer Revenues but reserves the right to issue Bonds for such purpose as 
necessary. Such Water Bonds shall be issued from time to time and with such terms provided by 
resolutions of the Authority adopted from time to time to supplement and to implem ent this General 
Bond Resolution as hereinafter provided. 
Section 302. Terms of Bonds. (a) (l) Each of the Sewer Bonds authorized pursuant to 
Section 301 shall be designated "Sewer Revenue Bond, Series_," with the year ofissuance being 
inserted in the designation. Each Sewer Bond shall be dated as of such date, shall mature on such 
date or dates, shall bear interest at such rate or varying rates per annum not exceeding any lim itation 
prescribed by law and shall be in such initial Mode as may be fixed or determined by resolution of 
the Authority adopted prior lo the day of delivery thereof and payment therefor. 
(2) Each of the Water Bonds authorized pursuant to Section 301 shall be designated "Water 
Revenue Bond, Series __ ," with the year of issuance being ins erted in the designation. Each 
Water Bond shall be dated as of such date, shall mature on such elate or dates, shall bear interest at 
such rate or varying rates per annum not exceeding any limitation prescribed by law and shall be in 
such initial Mode as may be fixed or determined by resolution of the Authority adopted prior to the 
day of deiivery thereof and payment therefor. 
(b) Each of the Additional Bonds authorized pursuant to Section 317 of the General Bond 
Resolution shall be desi gnated "Sewer Revenue Bond, Series _" or "Sewer Revenue Bond, Parity 
Series _" or "Water Revenue Bond, Series _" or "Water Revenue Bond, Parity Series _." 
as appropriate. Each Additional Bond shall be dated as of such date, shall mature on such date or 
dates, shall bear interest at such rate or varying rates per annum not exceeding any limitation thereon

Section 305. Mode Features. 
(a) Flexible Mode. 
( I) Determination ofFlexible Rates. The Remarketing Agent shall determine the 
Flexible Rates as provided in the form of Flexible Bonds and shall notify the Paying Agent 
thereof electronically orby telephone not laterthan I :00 P .M., promptly confirmed in ,,Ti ting 
(including by facsimile), on the Effective Date. The Remarketing Agent shall give prompt 
wrinen notice of the Flexible Rates to the Trustee, the Bank and the Authority. Each 
determination and redetermination of the Flexible Rate shall be conclusive and binding on 
the Authority, the Trustee, the Paying Agent, the .Bank and the Bondholders. If for any 
reason the Remarketing Agent fails to determine the Flexible Rate or Rate Period for any 
Bond while in the Flexible Mode, or if for any reason such manner of determination shall be 
determined to be invalid or unenforceable, that Bond, until changed as provided in tl1e form 
ofFlexible Bond in Exhibit A hereof, shall be deemed to be in the Daily Mode and the Daily 
Rate shall be equal to the lesser of (A) the PSA Municipal Index and (B) the Maximum 
Interest Rate. 
In determining the Flexible Rate and rernarketing Bonds in the Flexible Mode, the 
Remarketi ng Agent shal I, in addition to the constraints set forth in Exhibit A, ( l) not offer 
Rate Periods extending beyond the Letter of Credit Terminaiion Date, less five (5) days, (2) 
not offer Rate Periods applicable to Bonds to be converted extending beyond the day 
preceding any scheduled conversion of the Bonds to any other Mode or the final maturity of 
the Bonds, and (3) follow any written directions of the Authority Officer, not inconsist ent 
with the preceding clauses (1) and (2), as to the Rate Periods and interest rates to be made 
available. The Authority, the Trustee, the Paying Agent and the Remarketing Agent shall 
cooperate to ensure compliance with this requirement.

Section 307. Req1arketin!l of Bonds Tendered. 
(a) General. Subject to the provisions of Section 1301 (a)(5). the Remarketing Agent 
shall solicit offers to purchase and use its best efforts to find a purchaser for Tendered Bonds. Any 
such purchase shall be made by payment of the Purchase Price in immediately available funds to the 
Paying Agent at the time specified in Section 306(a)(2). By (I) 2: 15 P.M., in the case of Bonds that 
are to be in the Flexible Mode or the Daily Mode immediately after the Purchase Date, or (2) 2:00 
P.M .. in the case of Bonds that are to be in the Weekly or Multi annual Mode immediately after the 
Purchase Date, Bonds remarketed under this Section shall be made available on the Purchase Date 
by the Paying Agent to the purchasers thereof (in the case of Bonds in the Flexible Mode or the Daily 
Mode, delivered by the Paying Agent to the Remarketing Agent) and shall be registered in the 
manner directed by the recipient thereof, provided that such Bonds shall not be delivered unless and 
until the Paying Agent has received the Purchase Price therefor. except that Bonds in the Flexible 
Mode or the Daiiy Mode may be delivered against a window receipt of the Remarketing Agent 
guaranteeing same day payment in immediately available funds. Bonds not remarketed shall be held 
by the Paying Agent. 
Upon receipt by the Paying Agent ofnotice from the Remarketing Agent that a purchaser has 
been found for Pledged Bonds held by the Paying Agent, the Paying Agent shall register and deliver 
such Bonds to such purchaser (at which time such Bonds shall cease to be Pledged Bonds) upon 
receipt by the Paying Agent of the Purchase Price of such Bonds. The Paying Agent shall give notice 
to the Bank if and to the extent that the Paying Agent has received the proceeds of remarketing of 
any Pledged Bonds promptly of the receipt thereol: The Paying Agent shall immediately notify 
(subsequently confirmed in writing) the Remarketing Agent whenever (1) it is prohibited from 
registering and delivering Bonds pursuant to this Resolution and (2) if the Paying Agent has been 
so prohibited, upon the restoration of its power hereunder to register and deliver Bonds. Pledged 
Bonds shall be delivered to and held by the Paying Agent as agent for the Bank and shall not be 
subsequently transferred or assigned by the Bank except as provided in tl1is Section 307. No Bonds 
that are automatically converted to the Daily Mode after failure of an optional conversion from one 
34 Mode to another (or from one Rate Period to another in the Multi annual Mode) shall be remarketed 
until the Paying Agent notifies the Remarketing Agent (promptly confirmed in writing) that such 
Bonds are supported by a Letter of Credit meeting the requirements of Section 1404.

Section 315 . Issuance of Bonds and Disbursements of Proceeds of Sale and Other Funds. 
An amount not exceeding the aggregate principal amount of Bonds particularly authorized by the 
General Bond Resolution or Supplemental Resolution of the Authority may be executed by or on 
behalf of the Authority and may be delivered to the Trustee for authentication by the Trustee or to 
the Registrar for authentication of any Series by the Registrar. Thereupon the Bonds shall be 
authenticated by the Trustee or by the Registrar. If authenticated by the Registrar, the Registrar shall 
deliver the authenticated Bonds to the Trustee, to th.e Authority or upon its order. Upon fulfillment 
of the conditions hereinatler set forth, the Bonds shall be delivered by the Trustee to the Authority 
or upon its order. The proceeds of sale of such Bonds, including accrued interest, together with other 
fw1ds, if any, held by the Authority and not pledged or otherwise committed for a specific purpose 
shall be paid by the Authority simultaneously with the issuance of Bonds as provided in a 
Supplemental Resolution to be adopted prior to the authorization and the delivery of such Bonds.

Section 310 . No Recourse on the Bonds. No recourse shall be had for the payment of the 
principal of or the interesl on any Bonds or for any claim based thereon or on the General Bond 
Resolution against any member or officer of the Authority or any person executing the Bonds. The 
Bonds arc not and shall not be in any way a debt or liability of'the State of New Jersey or of any 
county or municipality and do not and shall not create or constitute any indebtedness. liability or 
obligation of such State or of any county or mtmicipality, either legal, moral or otherwise. 
Section 31 1. lnterchangeabilitv of Bonds. At the option of the Registered Owner, Bonds 
may be exchanged upon surrender thereof at the principal office of the Registrar with a written 
instrument or transfer satisfactory to the Re-gistrar duly executed by the Registered Owner thereof 
or his attorney duly authorized in writing for an equal aggregate principal amount of Bonds of the 
same Series, designation. maturity and interest rate of any other authorized denominations. 
Section 312 . Ownership of Bonds and Effect of Registration. Except as otherwise provided 
in Article XIII, the Authority and any Fiduciary may treat and may consider the person in whose 
name any Bond for the time being shall be registered as the holder and the absolute owner thereof; 
whether such Bond shall be overdue or not, for the purpose of receiving payment of the principal or 
the Redemption Price thereof or interest thereon and for all other purposes whatsoever. Payment of 
or on account of the principal or the Redemption Price ofor the interest on such Bond shall be made 
only to or upon the order of such Registered Owner thereof, but such registration may be changed 
or may be discharged as provided herein. All payments made as provided in this Section shall be 
valid and effectual to satisfy and to discharge the liability upon the several Bonds to the extent of the 
sum or sums so paid.

Operating Expenses of the Water System and to the rights of the llolders of the First Lien Water 
Bonds. all Water Revenues. moneys, securities and funds held or set aside or to be held or set aside 
pursuant to the General Bond Resolution for the Water Bonds by any Fiduciary or in any fund 
created by Section 502(a)(2) of the General Bond Resolution are hereby pledged to secure the 
payment of the principal or the Redemption Price of and the interest on the Water Bonds. This 
pledge shall be valid and binding from and after the time of the delivery by the Trustee of the first 
Water Bond authenticated and delivered under the General Bond Resolution. The Water Revenues 
and other moneys, securities and funds so pledged and then or thereafter received by the Authority 
immediately shall be subject to the lien of such pledge without any physical delivery or further act. 
The lien of such pledge and the obligation to perform the contractual provisions hereby made shall 
have priority over any or all other obligations and liabilities of the Authority, except as provided in 
Section 205 herein. The lien of such pledge shall be valid and binding as against all parties having 
claims of any kind in tort, contract or otherwise against the Authority irrespective of whether such 
parties have notice thereof.

City, who shall apply the same to such payment.

Bond Sinking Fund or the Water Bond Sinking Fund pursuant to Section 507 shall be set aside upon 
receipt in the account therein with respect to which such payment is a reimbursement. Moneys paid 
into the Sewer Bond Sinking Fund or the Water Bond Sinking Fund pursuant to paragraph (b) of 
this Section on account of any particular Sinking Fund Installment shall be set aside in the account 
therein maintained for the particular Bonds entitled to such Sinking Fund Installment. Any interest 
or profit on any investment of moneys in any account in the Sewer Bond Sinking Fund or the Water 
Bond Sinking Fund shall be deposited by the Trustee notwithstanding the provisions of Section 1205 
in such account.

Section 603. 
�-rhe Authority shall construct and shall complete the acquisition and the

Section 604. Operation and Maintenance of System . The Authority at all times shall 
operate the System properly and in a sound and economical manner, shall maintain, shall preserve 
and shall keep it properly or shall cause it to be so maintained, preserved and kept with the 
appurtenances and every part and parcel thereof in good repair. working order and condition and 
from time to time shall make or shall cause to be made all necessary and proper repairs, replacements 
and renewals so that at all times the operation of the System may be properly and advantageously 
conducted.

Resolution shall require the Authority to complete the construction of any Sewer Project, Water

Additional Water Project orpart thereofis economically justified and is not prejudicial to the interest 
of the Authority or the Bondholders and (2) a Counsel's Opinion that abandonment of construction 
of such Sewer Project. Water Project, Additional Sewer Project, Additional Water Project or part 
thereof will not violate the provisions of the Sewerage System Service Contract or the Water System 
Service Contract. as applicable, or prejudice the rights of Bondholders thereunder and (3 J a

ARTICLE VII 
REDEMPTION OF BONDS 
Section 702. Selection ofB()nds to be Redeemed. lflcss than all of the Bonds of anv Series 
oflikc maturity then Outstanding are ro be redeemed. the Authority shall designate the at;ount of 
Bonds of each Series and each Mode to be redeemed, and ifless than all of the Outstanding Bonds 
of any Series or any Mode shall be called for redemption. the Bonds !o he so redeemed ;hall be 
selected hy the Trustee by lot (provided that any Pledged Bonds shall be redeemed prior to the 
redemption of any other Bonds) or as otherwise provided by Supplemental Resolution authorizing 
such Series. In selecting Bonds to be redeemed. the Trustee shall treat each Bond as consisting of 
the quotient of the amount of the principal value of such Bond divided by$ 1,000, provided that no 
Bond shall be redeemed which would result in the unredeemed portion thereof aggr�gating less than 
an Authorized Denomination. 
Section 703. �- When the Trustee shall be required or authorized or 
shall receive notict? from the Authority of its election to redeem Bonds of any seiies� the Trustee in 
accordance with the terms and provisions of the Bonds and of the General Bond Resolution shall 
select the Bonds to be redeemed and shail give notice in the name ofrhe Authority of the redemption 
of Bonds. Such notice shall specify the Series and the maturities of the Bonds to be redeemed, the 
redemption date and the place of the places where amounts due upon such redemption will be 
payable and, if less than ail of the Bonds of any like Series and maturity are to be redeemed, the 
letters and the numbers or other distinguishing marks of such Bonds so to be redeemed. In the case 
of a Bond to be redeemed in part only, such notice shall also specify the portion of the principal 
amount thereof to be redeemed. Such notice shall state further that on such date there shall become 
due and payable upon each Bond to be redeemed the Redemption Price thereof or the Redemption 
Pri ce of the specified portion of the principal thereofin the case ofa Registered Bond to be redeemed 
in part only, together with interest accrued to such date, and that from and after such date interest 
thereon shall cease to accrne and be payable. Such noti ce shall be given by the Trustee by mailing 
a copy of such notice, postage prepaid, not less than thirty (30) days before such redemption date. 
to the Registered Owner of any Bond all or a portion of which is to be redeemed at his last address. 
if any. appearing upon the registry books kept by the Registrar. but re ceipt of such mailing shall not 
70 be a condition precedent to such redemption and failure so to mail any such notice shall not affect 
the validity of any proceedings for the redemption of Bonds. If at the time of mailiug of any notice 
of redemption there shall not be on deposit with the Trustee moneys sufficient to redeem all the 
Bonds called for redemption. such notice shall state that such redemption is subject to the deposit 
of the redemption moneys with the Trustee not later than the opening of business on the redemption 
date and that such notice shall be ofno effect unless such moneys are so deposited. 
Section 704. ALLthoritv's Election to Redeem. The Authority shell give written notke to 
the Trustee of its election to redeem Bonds of any series and of the redemption date. Such notice 
shall be given at least sixty days prior to the redemption date or at such later date as shall be 
acceptable to the Trustee. In the event that the required notice of redemption shall have been given. 
the Authority shall and hereby covenants that it will pay to the Trustee prior to the redemption date 
an amount in cash that. in addition to any other moneys available therefor held by the Trustee. will 
be sufl1cient to redeem at the Redemption Price thereoL plus interest accrued to the redemption date. 
all of the Bonds that are to be redeemed. 
Section 705. Payment of Redeemed Bonds. Notice having been given in the manner 
provided in Section 703, the Bonds of any series or the portions thereof called for redemption and 
specified in such notice shall become due and payable on the redemption date specified in such

provided that no such removal of a Trustee by the Authority shall take effect until a successor shall

Section 1403. Termination of Letter of Credit. The Letter of Credit shall terminate in 
accordance with its terms on the Letter of Credit Termination Date. Upon such termination, the 
Trustee shall deliver the terminated Letter of Credit to the Bank, together with such certificates as 
may be required by the terms of the Letter of Credit. 
Section 1404. Substitution or Replacement of Letter of Credit . Upon satisfaction of the 
requirements set forth in, and subject to the requirements of, this Section 1404, the Authority may 
provide for an Alternate Security to replace the Letter of Credit then in effect. The approval by the 
Authority ofan assignment of all or a part of a Bank's commitment under the Letter of Credit or an 
Alternate Security shall be deemed the replacement of the Letter of Credit or Alternate Security, as 
the case may be, for the purposes of this Section 1404. Unless the Authority is permitted by Section 
305(d)(4) to terminate the Letter of Credit and elects to terminate the Letter of Credit, the 
Authority shall replace a Letter of Credit with an Alternate Security within forty-five (45) days of 
a reduction in the short-term ratings on any Bonds support�d by a Letter of Credit to ratings lower 
than those described in Section 1405( d). In no event, however, shall the Letter of Credit being 
replaced be terminated or released until (a) the Authority has given not less than forty-five ( 45) days' 
written notice to the Ttustee, the Paying Agent and the Remarketing Agent, and the Paying Agent 
has received the proceeds of all outstanding demands on the Letter of Credit being replaced, and (b) 
if any Bonds supported by the Letter of Credit being replaced are in the Daily Mode or the Weekly 
Mode, the Paying Agent has given not less than thirty (30) days' written notice of the termination or 
release of the Letter of Credit and the identity of the issuer of the Alternate Security which will 
replace the Letter of Credit to owners of such Bonds in the Daily Mode or the Weekly Mode and (c) 
if any of the Bonds supported by the LetterofCredit being replaced are in the Flexible Mode or the 
Multimmual Mode, the Letter of Credit shall in no event be terminated or released earlier than on

Prior to the provision of an Alternate Security or the replacement of any Letter of Credit, the 
Authority shall have delivered to the Trustee and the Paying Agent: (a) an opinion of counsel for the 
issuer of the Alternate Security to the effect that it constitutes a legal, valid and binding obligation 
of the issuer enforceable in accordance with its tern1s; (b) an opinion of Bond Counsel to the effect 
that the substitution does not impair the exclusion of the interest on the Bonds from gross income 
for purposes of federal income taxation, (c) an opinion of counsel for the issuer of the Alternate 
Security, counsel for the Authority or the Trustee to the effect that the Alternate Security meets the 
requirements of this Section 1404; (d) a certificate of the Bank that all amounts due under the Letter 
of Credit have been paid and that the Authority has fulfilled all 
Agreement; and (e) written evidence from the Rating Agencies. Bonds are rated by 
either of the Rating Agencies, that the replacement of the Letter of Credit will not in itself result in 
the reduction, suspension or withdrawal of the short-term rating or ratings on the Bonds to ratings 
lower than those required hy Section 1405( d).

event of default under the Bond Resolution as long as the Purchase Price is made available on the

the remaining term of this (Sewer] [Water] Bond. Notwithstanding the foregoing. the Fixed Rate 
so established shall not exceed the Maximum Rate. 
Each determination and redetermination of the Multiannual Rate shall be conclusive and 
binding on the Authority, the Trustee, the Paying Agent, the Bank and the Bondholders. 
Interest on this [Sewer] [Water] Bond shall be computed on the basis of a 360-day year 
consisting of twelve 30-day months. From and after the date on which this (Sewer] [Water] Bond 
becomes due, any unpaid principal will bear interest at the then Multiannual Rate until paid or duly 
provided for.

THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
SUPPLEMENTAL RESOLUTION AUTHORJZING THE ISSUANCE OF 
NOT TO EXCEED $157,000 ,000 WATER REVENUE BONDS, AND IN 
ANTICIPATION THEREOF, PROJECT NOTES, OF THE JERSEY CITY 
MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORJTY 
MOTIONED BY, 
SECONDED BY, 
COMMISSIONER NALLY 
COMMISSIONER HAR TYE 
WHEREAS, on January 28, 1998, the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority (the 
"Authority"), a public body corporate and politic of the State of New Jersey organized pursuant to 
the Municipal and County Utilities Authorities Law (N.J.S.A. 40:14B-1 .e1 �-) (the "Act") 
adopted a resolution entitled, "Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Revenue Bonds of the 
Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority", as amended and supplemented (the "General Bond 
Resolution"); 
and, 
WHEREAS, Section 317 of the General Bond Resolution provides for the issuance by the 
Authority of Additional Water Bonds for the purpose of raising funds for Additional Water 
Projects, as defined therein, upon the terms and conditions set forth in the General Bond 
Resolution; 
and, 
WHEREAS, on April 28, 2011, the Authority adopted a resolution entitled, "Resolution 
Authorizing the Issuance of Water Project Notes of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority" 
as amended and supplemented (the •'Water Project Note Resolution"); 
and, 
WHEREAS, Section 311 of the Water Project Note Resolution provides for the issuance 
by the Authority of Additional Project Notes (as defined in the Water Project Note Resolution) for 
the purpose of raising funds for the Cost of the acquisition of construction of part or parts of the 
System (as defined in the Water Project Note Resolution) (each, an "Additional Water Project"), 
upon the terms and conditions set forth in the Water Project Note Resolution;

Financing Program'') and (ii) long-term loans to be made by the I-Bank and the State of New 
Jersey, acting by and through the New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection (the 
"State") to the Township and evidenced by the issuance by the Authorily of one or more series of 
Bonds, through the Kew Jersey Water Bank (each such series of Bonds, the "NJID Bonds"); 
and, 
WHEREAS, any Project not financed through the New Jersey Water Bank will be 
financed through the issuance of one or more series of Bonds and, in anticipation thereof, one or 
more series of Project Notes, if applicable, sold at competitive or negotiated sale on the tenns and 
conditions set forth herein ( each such series of Bonds, the "Market Bonds"); 
and, 
WHEREAS, the Authority desires to specify and detennine certain terms and conditions 
of the Obligations being issued; 
NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED BY THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL 
UTILITIES AUTHORITY, and the members and commissioners thereof as follows:

Section 10 1. Short Title. This resolution may hereinafter be cited by the Authority and 
is hereinafter sometimes referred to as the "2023 Supplemental Water Resolution".

specifically defined herein or unless the context clearly requires otherwise, have the meanings 
assigned to such tenns in the General Bond Resolution.

ARTICLE II 
Section 201. Authority for 2023 Supplemental Waler Resolution. This 2023 
Supplemental Water Resolution is adopted pursuant to the Act and the General Bond Resolution 
and Water Project Note Resolution, as applicable, and the Authority has ascertained and hereby 
determines that each and every act, matter, thing or course of conduct as to which provision is 
made in this 2023 Supplemental Water Resolution, is appropriate in order to cany out and 
effectuate the purposes of the Authority in accordance with the Act and the General Bond 
Resolution and Water Project Note Resolution, as applicable, to further secure the payment of the 
principal of and interest on the Obligations. 
Section 202. Bonds to Constitute Additional Water Bonds. The Bonds shall constitute 
Additional Water Bonds as such term is defined in the General Bond Resolution and shall be issued 
pursuant to and in accordance with Section 317 of the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 203. Project Notes to Constitute Additional Project Notes. The Project Notes 
shall constitute Additional Project Notes as such term is defined in the Water Project Not 
Resolution and shall be issued pursUW1t to and in accordance with Section J 11 of the Water Project 
Note Resolution. 
Section 204. 2023 Supplemental Water Resolution to Constitute Contract Amongst 
Purchasers of Bonds, the Aulhoritv and the Trustee. In consideration of the purchase and 
acceptance of the Bonds, the provisions of this 2023 Supplemental Water Resolution applicable to 
the Bonds shall be deemed to be and shall constitute a contract between the purchasers of such 
Bonds, the Authority, and the Trustee; the pledge made in this 2023 Supplemental Water 
Resolution and the covenants and agreements herein set forth to be performed by or on behalf of 
the Authority shall be for the equal benefit, protection and security of the holders of the Bonds, all 
of which, regardless of the time or times of their issue or maturity, shall be of equal rank without 
preference, priority or distinction of any of the Bonds over any other thereof except as expressly 
provided in or pursuant to the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 205. 2023 Supplemental Water Resolution to Constitute Contract Amongst 
Purchasers of Project Notes, the Authority and the Trustee. In consideration of the purchase 
and acceptance of the Project Notes, the provisions of this 2023 Supplemental Water Resolution 
applicable to the Project Notes shall be deemed to be and shall constitute a contract between the 
purchasers of such Project Notes, the Authority, and the Trustee; the pledge made in this 2023 
Supplemental Water Resolution and the covenants and agreements herein set forth to be performed 
by or on behalf of the Authority shall be for the equal benefit, protection and security of tbe holders 
of the Project Notes, a11 ofwbjc}1, regardless of the time or times of their issue or maturity, shall

Section 103. Incorporation of General Bond Resolution. This 2023 Supplemental 
Water Resolution supplements and amends the General Bond Resolution where applicable. With 
respect to the provisions hereof applicable to the Bonds, the General Bond Resolution is 
incorporated herein by reference thereto. 
Section 104. Incorporation of Water Project Note Resolution. This 2023 
Supplemental Water Resolution supplements and amends the Water Project Note Resolution 
where applicable. With respect to the provisions hereof applicable to the Project Notes, the Water 
Project Note Resolution is incorporated herein by reference thereto. 
Section 105. Sevcrability of Invalid Pro, 1isions . If any one or more of the covenants or 
agreements provided in this 2023 Supplemental Water Resolution, on the part of the Authority or 
the Trustee, to be performed should be contrary to law, then such covenant or covenants, agreement 
or agreements, shall be deemed separable from the remaining covenants and agreements and shall 
in no way affect the validity of the other provisions of the 2023 Supplemental Water Resolution 
or of nny Obligation. 
(End of Article [)

Section 206. Estimated Cost of the Projects. The Authority hereby detennines that the 
aggregate estimated Cost of the Projects shall not exceed $ I 57,000,000, inclusive of any original 
issue discount, capitalized interest and all reserves, and exclusive of accrued interest on any 
Obligations. 
(End of Article II)
