ARTICLE III 
Section 301. Authorization and Purpose of the Bonds. The Bonds oftbe Authority in 
the principal amount of not to exceed $157,000,000 are hereby authorized to be issued in one or 
more series for the purpose of paying a portion of the Cost of the Projects. 
Section 302. Sale and Terms of Bonds. The Executive Director, Chairperson and Vice 
Chairperson of the Authority (each, an "Authorized Officer") are hereby designated a,; the 
individuals who shall have the power to determine, among other things (a) the principal amount 
of each series of Bonds to be issued, in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed $157,000,000, 
(b) the time, date, series, denomination(s), maturity dates, redemption provisions, manner of sale 
(including which Bonds are sold through the New Jersey Water Bank) and place of payment of 
each series of Bonds, (c) the rate of interest for each series of Bonds, and (d) such other terms and 
conditions as may be necessary or related to the sale of each series of the Bonds. The Authorized 
Officers are hereby designated as the individuals who shall have the power to sell each series of 
the Bonds, subject to the conditions and limitations set forth herein. 
Section 303. New Jersey Water Bank Bonds. (A) The Authorized Officers are hereby 
designated as the individuals who shall have the power to detennine which Projects shall be 
financed through the New Jersey Water Bank. Each series of Bonds sold through the New Jersey 
Water Bank shall be comprised of a "Water Revenue Bond, Series 20_A" (with such series 
designation to reflect the calendar year of issuance of such bond) (each, a "Series A Bond") to be 
issued by the Authority to the l•Bank pursuant to the terms of each certain Loan Agr eement, dated 
as of the date of issuance of each such Series A Bond, by and between the Authority and the J. 
Bank (each, a "Trust Loan Agreement"), and a "Water Revenue Bond, Series 20_B" (with such 
series designation to reflect the calendar year of issuance of such bond) ( each, a "Series B Bond") 
to be issued by the Authority to the State pursuant to the tenns of each certain Loan Agreement, 
dated as of the date of issuance of each such Series B Bond, by and between the Authority and the 
State (each, a "Fund Loan Agreement" and, together with the Trust Loan Agreement, the "Loan 
Agreements"). 
(B) The Authorized Officers are hereby authorized to determine, pursuant to the terms 
and conditions established by the l•Bank and the State under the Loan Agreements and the terms 
and conditions hereof, the following additional items with respect to each series ofNJIB Bonds: 
(i) The aggregate principa l amounts of each Series A Bond and each Series B 
Bond to be issued in an aggregate amount not to exceed $157,000,000 less 
any Bonds previously issued pursuant to this 2023 Supplemental Water 
Resolution;

Section 503. Covenant of Authority as lo Compliance with Federal Tax Matten. 
The Authority hereby covenants that it v.ill take all actions within its control that arc necessary to 
assure that interest on any tax-exempt Obligations is cxcludable from gross income under the 
Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended (the "Code"), and the Authority will refrain from 
taking any action that would adversely affect the exclusion of interest on any tax-exempt 
Obligations from gross income under the provisions of the Code. 
Section S04. Declaration of Int ent. (A) The Aut hority reasonably expects to reimburse 
its expenditure of Co sis of the Projects paid prior to the issuance of the Obligations with proceeds 
of its Obligations. This resolution is intended to be and hereby is a declaration of the Authority's 
official intent to reimburse the expenditure of Costs of the Projects paid prior to the issuance of 
the Obligations with the proceeds of one or more borrowings to be incurred by the Authority, in 
accordance with Treasury Regulations §150 -2. The aggregate maximum principal amount of the 
Obligations expected to be issued to finance the Projects is not to exceed $157 ,000,000. The Costs 
of the Projects to be reimbursed with the proceeds of the Obligations will be "capital expenditllres" 
in accordance with the meaning of Section 150 of the Code. 
(B) �o reimbursement allocation will employ an "abusive arbitrage device" under 
Treaswy Regulations § 1.14 8-10 to avoid the arbitrage restrictions or to avoid the restrictions under 
Sections 14 2 through 147, inclusive, of the Code. The proceeds of the Obligations used to 
reimburse the Authority for Costs of the Projects, or funds corresponding to such amounts, will 
not be used in a manner that results in the creation of "replacement proceeds", including "sinking 
funds", "pledged funds" or funds subject to a "negative pledge" (as such tenns are defined in 
Treasury Regulations §1.14 8-1), of the Obligations or another issue of debt obligations of the 
Authority, other than amou nts deposited inlo a "bona fide debt service fund" (as defined in 
Treaswy Regulations § 1. 148-1 ).

SECONDED BY: COMMISSIONER NALLY 
WHEREAS, on January 28, 1998, the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority (the 
“Authority”, a public body corporate and politic of the State of New Jersey organized pursuant to the Municipal and County Utilities Authorities Law (N.J.S.A. 40:14B-1 et seq.) (the “Act”) 
adopted a resolution entitled, “Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Revenue Bonds of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority”, as amended and supplemented (the “General Bond 
Resolution”); and 
WHEREAS, Section 317 of the General Bond Resolution provides for the issuance by the Authority of Additional Water Bonds for the purpose of raising funds for Additional Water 
Projects, as defined therein, upon the terms and conditions set forth in the General Bond Resolution; and 
WHEREAS, on April 28, 2011, the Authority adopted a resolution entitled, “Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Water Project Notes of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority” 
as amended and supplemented (the “Water Project Note Resolution”); and 
WHEREAS, Section 311 of the Water Project Note Resolution provides for the issuance 
by the Authority of Additional Project Notes (as defined in the Water Project Note Resolution) for the purpose of raising funds for the Cost of the acquisition of construction of part or parts of the 
System (as defined in the Water Project Note Resolution) (each, an “Additional Water Project”), upon the terms and conditions set forth in the Water Project Note Resolution; and 
WHEREAS, the Authority desires to undertake the various projects described more particularly on Exhibit A, attached hereto (cach, a “Project” and collectively, the “Projects”; and 
WHEREAS, in accordance with the provisions of Section 317 of the General Bond 
Resolution, the Authority intends to hereby authorize one or more series of Additional Water Bonds of the Authority designated as “Water Revenue Bonds, Series 20__” (with such series 
designation to reflect the calendar year of issuance of each such series of bonds) (collectively, the

ARTICLET 
Section 101. Short Title. This resolution may hereinafter be cited by the Authority and 
is hereinafter sometimes referred to as the “2024 Supplemental Water Resolution”. 
Section 102. Definitions. Terms which are used as defined terms herein shall, unless 
specifically defined herein or unless the context clearly requires otherwise, have the meanings assigned to such terms in the General Bond Resolution. 
“Act” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Additional Water Project” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Authority” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Authority Consultants” shall be as defined in Section 303(G). 
“Authorized Officer” shall be as defined in Section 302. 
“Bond Insurer” shall be as defined in Section 304(F). 
“Bonds” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Certificate” shall be as defined in Section 304(A). 
“Code” shall be as defined in Section 503. 
“Construction Financing Program” shall have the meaning given to such term in the recitals hereof. 
“Consultants” shall be as defined in Section 303(G). 
“Cost” shall have the meaning given to such term in the Act. 
“Escrow Agreement” means the Escrow Agreement by and among the I-Bank, the State, 
the Authority, the Trustee and the escrow agent named therein. 
“Financing Documents” shall be as defined in Section 303(G).

“Bonds”), in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed $61,616,000, to provide for the long- 
term financing of the Projects; and 
WHEREAS, in anticipation of the issuance of the Bonds, and in accordance with the provisions of Section 311 of the Water Project Note Resolution, the Authority intends to hereby 
authorize one or more series of Additional Project Notes of the Authority designated as “Project 
Notes, Series 20__” (with such series designation to reflect the calendar year of issuance of such series of project notes) (collectively, the “Project Notes” and, together with the Bonds, the 
“Obligations”), in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed $61,616,000, to provide for the short-term financing of the Projects; and 
WHEREAS, the Authority may obtain financing for one or more Projects through 
participation in the low-interest rate environmental infrastructure financing program (the “New Jersey Water Bank”) of the New Jersey Infrastructure Bank (the “I-Bank”); and 
WHEREAS, any Project financed through the New Jersey Water Bank will be financed 
with the proceeds of (3) one or more short-term loans to be made by the I-Bank to the Authority and evidenced by the issuance by the Authority of one or more series of Project Notes, issued 
pursuant to the Water Bank Construction Financing Program of the I-Bank (the “Construction Financing Program”) and (ii) long-term loans to be made by the I-Bank and the State of New 
Jersey, acting by and through the New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection (the “State”) to the Township and evidenced by the issuance by the Authority of one or more series of 
Bonds, through the New Jersey Water Bank (cach such series of Bonds, the “N/IB Bonds”); and 
WHEREAS, any Project not financed through the New Jersey Water Bank will be 
financed through the issuance of one or more series of Bonds and, in anticipation thereof, one or more series of Project Notes, if applicable, sold at competitive or negotiated sale on the terms and 
conditions set forth herein (cach such series of Bonds, the “Market Bonds”); and 
WHEREAS, the Authority desires to specify and determine certain terms and conditions 
of the Obligations being issued; 
NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED BY THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL

Section 103. Incorporation of General Bond Resolution. This 2024 Supplemental 
Water Resolution supplements and amends the General Bond Resolution where applicable. With 
respect to the provisions hereof applicable to the Bonds, the General Bond Resolution is 
incorporated herein by reference thereto. 
Section 104. Incorporation of Water Project Note Resolution. This 2024 
Supplemental Water Resolution supplements and amends the Water Project Note Resolution 
where applicable. With respect to the provisions hereof applicable to the Project Notes, the Water 
Project Note Resolution is incorporated herein by reference thereto. 
Section 105. Severability of Invalid Provisions. If any one or more of the covenants or 
agreements provided in this 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution, on the part of the Authority or 
the Trustee, to be performed should be contrary to law, then such covenant or covenants, agreement or agreements, shall be deemed separable from the remaining covenants and agreements and shall 
in no way affect the validity of the other provisions of the 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution or of any Obligation. 
(End of Article I)

Section 206. Estimated Cost of the Projects. The Authority hereby determines that the aggregate estimated Cost of the Projects shall not exceed $61,616,000, inclusive of any original 
issue discount, capitalized interest and all reserves, and exclusive of accrued interest on any Obligations.

ARTICLE II 
Section 201. Authority for 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution, This 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution is adopted pursuant to the Act and the General Bond Resolution 
and Water Project Note Resolution, as applicable, and the Authority has ascertained and hereby determines that each and every act, matter, thing or course of conduct as to which provision is 
made in this 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution, is appropriate in order to carry out and effectuate the purposes of the Authority in accordance with the Act and the General Bond 
Resolution and Water Project Note Resolution, as applicable, to further secure the payment of the principal of and interest on the Obligations. 
Section 202. Bonds to Constitute Additional Water Bonds. The Bonds shall constitute 
Additional Water Bonds as such term is defined in the General Bond Resolution and shall be issued 
pursuant to and in accordance with Section 317 of the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 203. Project Notes to Constitute Additional Project Notes. The Project Notes 
shall constitute Additional Project Notes as such term is defined in the Water Project Not 
Resolution and shall be issued pursuant to and in accordance with Section 311 of the Water Project 
Note Resolution. 
Section 204. 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution to Constitute Contract Amongst Purchasers of Bonds, the Authority and the Trustee. In consideration of the purchase and 
acceptance of the Bonds, the provisions of this 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution applicable to the Bonds shall be deemed to be and shall constitute a contract between the purchasers of such 
Bonds, the Authority, and the Trustee; the pledge made in this 2024 Supplemental Water 
Resolution and the covenants and agreements herein set forth to be performed by or on behalf of the Authority shall be for the equal benefit, protection and security of the holders of the Bonds, all of which, regardless of the time or times of their issue or maturity, shall be of equal rank without 
preference, priority or distinction of any of the Bonds over any other thereof except as expressly provided in or pursuant to the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 205. 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution to Constitute Contract Amongst 
Purchasers of Project Notes, the Authority and the Trustee. In consideration of the purchase 
and acceptance of the Project Notes, the provisions of this 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution 
applicable to the Project Notes shall be deemed to be and shall constitute a contract between the 
purchasers of such Project Notes, the Authority, and the Trustee; the pledge made in this 2024 
Supplemental Water Resolution and the covenants and agreements herein set forth to be performed 
by or on behalfofthe Authority shall be for the equal benefit, protection and security of the holders 
of the Project Notes, all of which, regardless of the time or times of their issue or maturity, shall

ARTICLE III 
Section 301. Authorization and Purpose of the Bonds. The Bonds of the Authority in 
the principal amount of not to exceed $61,616,000 are hereby authorized to be issued in one or more series for the purpose of paying a portion of the Cost of the Projects. 
Section 302. Sale and Terms of Bonds. The Executive Director, Chairperson and Vice 
Chairperson of the Authority (cach, an “Authorized Officer”) are hereby designated as the 
individuals who shall have the power to determine, among other things (a) the principal amount of cach series of Bonds to be issued, in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed $61,616,000, (b) the time, date, series, denomination(s), maturity dates, redemption provisions, manner of sale 
(including which Bonds are sold through the New Jersey Water Bank) and place of payment of each series of Bonds, (c) the rate of interest for cach series of Bonds, and (d) such other terms and 
conditions as may be necessary or related to the sale of each series of the Bonds. The Authorized Officers are hereby designated as the individuals who shall have the power to sell each series of 
the Bonds, subject to the conditions and limitations set forth herein. 
Section 303. New Jersey Water Bank Bonds, (A) The Authorized Officers are hereby 
designated as the individuals who shall have the power to determine which Projects shall be financed through the New Jersey Water Bank. Each series of Bonds sold through the New Jersey 
Water Bank shall be comprised of a “Water Revenue Bond, Series 20__A” (with such series designation to reflect the calendar year of issuance of such bond) (each, a “Series A Bond”) to be 
issued by the Authority to the I-Bank pursuant to the terms of each certain Loan Agreement, dated as of the date of issuance of each such Series A Bond, by and between the Authority and the I- Bank (each, a “I-Bank Loan Agreement”), and a‘ Water Revenue Bond, Series 20__B” (with such 
series designation to reflect the calendar year of issuance of such bond) (cach, a “Series B Bond”) to be issued by the Authority to the State pursuant to the terms of each certain Loan Agreement, 
dated as of the date of issuance of each such Series B Bond, by and between the Authority and the 
State (cach, a “Fund Loan Agreement” and, together with the I-Bank Loan Agreement, the “Loan Agreements”), 
(B) The Authorized Officers are hereby authorized to determine, pursuant to the terms and conditions established by the I-Bank and the State under the Loan Agreements and the terms and conditions hereof, the following additional items with respect to each series of NJIB Bonds:

Section 304. Market Bonds, (A) Each series of Market Bonds may be sold at public or 
private sale as determined by an Authorized Officer. If sold at private sale, such series of Market Bonds may be sold to an underwriter or other members of the banking community as may be appointed by an Authorized Officer (collectively, the Underwriter”). Such Authorized Officers 
are each hereby authorized to execute a Purchase Agreement (as defined herein) or other similar document, if any, in connection with the sale of each series of Market Bonds and award each series 
of Market Bonds to the Underwriter, with such award being evidenced by the execution of a 
certificate by an Authorized Officer (the “Certificate”}. The Purchase Agreement, if any, and the Certificate shall determine the terms and conditions relating to the sale of each series of Market 
Bonds, including the rate of interest to be bome by such series of Market Bonds and the Underwriter’s discount, if any, which is payable to the underwriter in connection with the sale of 
such series of Market Bonds; provided however, that without the further authorization of the Authority, the Underwritar’s total compensation for the sale of any such series of Market Bonds shall not exceed $6.00 per $1,000 principal amount of such series of Market Bonds, it being

entitled to take pursuant to the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 305. No Recourse on the Bonds. No recourse shall be had for the payment of 
the principal of or the interest on the Bonds or for any claim based thereon or on the 2024 
Supplemental Water Resolution or the General Bond Resolution against any member or other officer of the Authority or any person executing the Bonds. The Bonds are not and shall not be in any way a debt or liability of the State of New Jersey or of any county or municipality and do not 
and shall not create or constitute any indebtedness, liability or obligation of said State or of any county or municipality, either legal, moral or otherwise. 
Section 306. Execution of Bonds. The Chairperson or Vice Chairperson of the 
Authority ishereby authorized to execute by the manual or facsimile signature each series of Bonds in the name and on behalf of the Authority attested by the manual or facsimile signature of its 
Secretary or Assistant Secretary, and following such execution and attestation, such bonds shall be delivered to the Trustee for authentication, and the Trustee is hereby authorized to authenticate 
such Bonds upon the fulfillment of the conditions set forth in Section 318 of the General Bond Resolution, 
Section 307. Appointment of Trustee. In accordance with the provisions of the General 
Bond Resolution, as amended, the Trustee is hereby appointed to serve as trustee of each series of 
Bonds and sball accept the carry out its obligations as such, including but not limited to, 
authentication of each series of Bonds.

Section 401. Autherization and Purpose of the Project Notes, The Project Notes of the Authority in the principal amount of not to exceed $61,616,000 are hereby authorized to be 
issued in one or more series, and in anticipation of the issuance of Bonds, for the purpose of paying a portion of the Cost of the Projects.

designated as the individuals who shall have the power to determine, among other things (a) the 
principal amount of each series of Project Notes to be issued, in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed $61,616,000, (b) the time, date, series, denomination(s), maturity dates, redemption 
provisions, manner of sale (including which Project Notes are sold through the New Jersey Water Bank) and place of payment of each series of Project Notes, (c) the rate of interest for each series 
of Project Notes, and (d) such other terms and conditions as may be necessary or related to the sale of each series of the Project Notes. The Authorized Officers are hereby designated as the individuals who shall have the power to sell each series of the Project Notes, subject to the 
conditions and limitations set forth herein.

relation to the execution and delivery of each series of NJIB Project Notes and the participation of the Authority in the Construction Financing Program.

or other officer of the Authority or any person executing the Project Notes. The Project Notes are not and shall not be in any way a debt or liability of the State of New Jersey or of any county or 
municipality and do not and shall not create or constitute any indebtedness, Liability or obligation of said State or of any county or municipality, either legal, moral or otherwise.

Section 501. Continuing Market Disclosure, (A} Solely for purposes of complying 
with Rule 15c2-12 of the Securities and Exchange Commission, as amended and interpreted from 
time to time (the “Rule”, provided that (1) the Obligations are not exempt from the Rule, (i) the 
I-Bank has determined that the Obligations are not exempt from the following requirements and/or 
Gii) the Obligations are not exempt from the following requirements in accordance with paragraph 
(d) of the Rule, for so long as the Obligations remain outstanding (unless the Obligations have 
been wholly defeased), the Authority shall provide for the benefit of the holders of the Obligations 
and the beneficial owners thereof all information required under the Rule. 
(B) If all or any part of the Rule ceases to be in effect for any reason, then the information required to be provided under this 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution, insofar as 
the provision of the Rule no longer in effect required the provision of such information, shall no longer be required to be provided. 
(C) The Executive Director is hereby authorized to enter into additional written 
contracts or undertakings to implement the Rule and is further authorized to amend such contracts or undertakings or the undertakings set forth in this 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution, 
provided such amendment is, in the opinion of nationally recognized bond counsel, in compliance with the Rule or would have been in compliance with the Rule if such amended undertaking had 
been entered into at the time of the issuance of the applicable series of Obligations. 
(D) Inthe event that the Authority fails to comply with the Rule or the written contracts 
or undertakings specified in this 2024 Supplemental Water Resolution, the Authority shall not be 
liable for monetary damages, remedy being hereby specifically limited to specific performance of the Rule requirements or the written contracts or undertakings therefor.

authorizes each such Preliminary Official Statement and the information contained therein to be

Section 503. Covenant of Authority as te Compliance with Federal Tax Matters. 
The Authority hereby covenants that it will take all actions within its control that are necessary to 
assure that interest on any tax-exempt Obligations is excludable from gross income under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended (the “Code”), and the Authority will refrain from 
taking any action that would adversely affect the exclusion of interest on any tax-exempt 
Obligations from gross income under the provisions of the Code. 
Section 504. Declaration of Intent. (A) The Authority reasonably expects to reimburse its expenditure of Costs of the Projects paid prior to the issuance of the Obligations with proceeds 
of its Obligations. This resolution is intended to be and hereby is a declaration of the Authority's official intent to reimburse the expenditure of Costs of the Projects paid prior to the issuance of the Obligations with the proceeds of one or more borrowings to be incurred by the Authority, in 
accordance with Treasury Regulations §150-2. The aggregate maximum principal amount of the Obligations expected to be issued to finance the Projects is not to exceed $61,616,000. The Costs 
of the Projects to be reimbursed with the proceeds of the Obligations will be "capital expenditures" 
in accordance with the meaning of Section 150 of the Code. 
(B) No reimbursement allocation will employ an “abusive arbitrage device" under 
Treasury Regulations §1.148-10 to avoid the arbitrage restrictions or to avoid the restrictions under Sections 142 through 147, inclusive, of the Code. The proceeds of the Obligations used to 
reimburse the Authority for Costs of the Projects, or funds corresponding to such amounts, will not be used ina manner that results in the creation of "replacement proceeds", including "sinking 
funds", "pledged funds" or funds subject to a "negative pledge" (as such terms are defined in Treasury Regulations §1.148-1), of the Obligations or another issue of debt obligations of the

THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
SUPPLEMENTAL BOND RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE 
ISSUANCE OF NOT TO EXCEED $50,000,000 WATER REVENUE 
BONDS, SERIES 2025 OF THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL 
UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
MOTIONED BY: COMMISSIONER BROWN 
SECONDED BY: COMMISSIONER HARTYE 
WHEREAS, on January 28, 1998, the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority (the “ Authority”), a public body corporate and politic of the State of New Jersey organized pursuant to 
the Municipal and County Utilities Authorities Law (N.J.S.A. 40:14B-1 et seq.) (the “Act”) adopted a resolution entitled, “Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Revenue Bonds of the 
Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority”, as amended and supplemented (the “General Bond Resolution”); and 
WHEREAS, Section 317 of the General Bond Resolution provides for the issuance by the 
Authority of Additional Water Bonds for the purpose of raising funds for Additional Water 
Projects, each as defined in the General Bond Resolution, upon the terms and conditions set forth in the General Bond Resolution; and 
WHEREAS, the Authority desires to undertake the Additional Water Project described more particularly on Exhibit A, attached hereto (the “2025 Project”); and 
WHEREAS, in accordance with the provisions of Section 317 of the General Bond 
Resolution, the Authority intends to hereby authorize a series of Additional Water Bonds of the Authority designated as “Water Revenue Bonds, Series 2025” (the “2025 Bonds”) in an aggregate 
principal amount not to exceed $50,000,000, to provide for the financing of the 2025 Project; and 
WHEREAS, the Authority desires to specify and determine certain terms and conditions 
of the 2025 Bonds being issued; 
NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED BY THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL 
UTILITIES AUTHORITY, and the members and commissioners thereof as follows: 
3/25/28 
PAGE 3 OF 10 
THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES AUTHORITY 
RESOLUTION 
Section 104. Severability of Invalid Provisions, If any one or more of the covenants or 
agreements provided in this 2025 Supplemental Water Resolution, on the part of the Authority or 
the Trustee, to be performed should be contrary to law, then such covenant or covenants, agreement 
or agreements, shall be deemed separable from the remaining covenants and agreements and shall 
in no way affect the validity of the other provisions of the 2025 Supplemental Water Resolution 
or of any 2025 Bond. 
ARTICLE II 
Section 201. Authority for 2025 Supplemental Water Resolution, This 2025 
Supplemental Water Resolution is adopted pursuant to the Act and the General Bond Resolution and the Authority has ascertained and hereby determines that each and every act, matter, thing or 
course of conduct as to which provision is made in this 2025 Supplemental Water Resolution, is appropriate in order to carry out and effectuate the purposes of the Authority in accordance with 
the Act and the General Bond Resolution to further secure the payment of the principal of and interest on the 2025 Bonds. 
Section 202. 2025 Bonds to Constitute Additional Water Bonds. The 2025 Bonds shall 
constitute Additional Water Bonds as such term is defined in the General Bond Resolution and 
shall be issued pursuant to and in accordance with Section 317 of the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 203. 2025 Supplemental Water Resolution to Constitute Contract, In 
consideration of the purchase and acceptance of the 2025 Bonds, the provisions of this 2025 
Supplemental Water Resolution shall be deemed to be and shall constitute a contract between the purchasers of such bonds, the Authority, and the Trustee; the pledge made in this 2025 Supplemental Water Resolution and the covenants and agreements herein set forth to be performed 
by oron behalf of the Authority shall be for the equal benefit, protection and security of the holders of the 2025 Bonds, all of which, regardless of the time or times of their issue or maturity, shall be 
of equal rank without preference, priority or distinction of any of the 2025 Bonds over any other 
thereof except as expressly provided in or pursuant to the General Bond Resolution. 
Section 204, Estimated Cost of 2025 Project, The Authority hereby determines that the aggregate estimated Cost (as defined in the Act) of the 2025 Project shal! not exceed $50,000,000, 
inclusive of any original issue discount (but not original issue premium), capitalized interest and all reserves, and exclusive of accrued interest on the 2025 Bonds.

Water Resolution supplements and amends the General Bond Resolution. The General Bond Resolution is incorporated herein by reference thereto.

ARTICLE II 
Section 301. Authorization and Purpose of the 2025 Bonds, The 2025 Bonds of the 
Authority in the principal amount of not o exceed $50,000,000 are hereby authorized to be issued for the purpose of paying a portion of the Cost of the 2025 Project. Such 2025 Bonds shall be 
entitled “Water Revenue Bonds, Series 2025”. 
Section 302. Description and Terms of 2025 Bonds. 
(A) Saleand Terms of 2025 Bonds, The Executive Director or the Chairperson of the 
Authority are each hereby designated as the individuals who shall have the power to determine, among other things (2) the principal amount of 2025 Bonds to be issued, in an aggregate principal 
amount not to exceed $50,000,000, (b) the time, date, series, denomination(s), maturity dates, 
redemption provisions, manner of sale and place of payment of the 2025 Bonds, (c) the rate of interest for such 2025 Bonds, provided such average rate shal! not exceed 7.00%, and (4) such other terms and conditions as may be necessary or related to the sale of the 2025 Bonds. The 
Executive Director or the Chairperson of the Authority are hereby designated as the individuals who shall have the power to sell the 2025 Bonds, subject to the following limitations. The 2025 
Bonds of the Authority may be sold to an underwriter or other members of the banking community as may be appointed by an Authority Officer (collectively, the “Underwriter”). The Authority 
Officers are each hereby authorized to execute a Purchase Agreement or other similar document, 
ifany, in connection with the sale of the 2025 Bonds and award the 2025 Bonds to the Underwriter, with such award being evidenced by the execution of acertificate by the Executive Director or the 
Chairperson (the “Certificate”). The Purchase Agreement, if any, and the Certificate shall determine the terms and conditions relating to the sale of the 2025 Bonds, including the rate of 
interest to be bone by the 2025 Bonds and the Underwriter's discount, if any, which is payable to the underwriter in connection with the sale of the 2025 Bonds; provided however, that without the 
further authorization of the Authority, the Underwriter's total compensation for the public offering of the 2025 Bonds shall not exceed $6.00 per $1,000 principal amount of such 2025 Bonds, it 
being understood that the structure of this financing may include an Underwriter’s discount, original issue discount or premium above the par amount of the 2025 Bonds. The Purchase 
Agreement, if any, and the Certificate shall contain such other terms and conditions as shall be deemed to be necessary in connection with the sale of the 2025 Bonds. The Certificate shall be 
presented to the members of the Authority at the next regular meeting following such sale and award as evidence of the terms and details of the sale of the 2025 Bonds. 
The 2025 Bonds shall be in substantially the form described 
and contained in the General Bond Resolution.

of said State or of any county or municipality, either legal, moral or otherwise.

such Bonds upon the fulfillment of the applicable conditions set forth in Section 318 of the General Bond Resolution.

Bond Resolution, as amended, the Trustee is hereby appointed to serve as trustee of the 2025 Bonds and shall accept the carry out its obligations as such, including but not limited to, authentication of the 2025 Bonds.

Cn&i:r'lll&lll AMENDMENT No . 1 TO SEWER SERVICE CONTRACT 
THIS AMENDMENT No . 1 TO THE SEWER SERVICE CONTRACT made and 
dated as of the fi rst day of May, 20 03 (t he "Amendment 11), is 
between THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIP AL UTILITIES AUTHORITY (h erein 
refer red to as the "Authorit y" ) , a publ ic body po litic and 
corpora te , duly orga ni zed and exist ing under the Mu nic ipal and 
County Ut ili ties Auth orities Law of New Jersey, Chap ter 183 of 
the la ws of 1957 of New Jersey, and the acts amendatory thereof 
and supplemental thereto, (h erein refe rred to as the "Act 11 ) and 
THE CITY OF JERSEY CITY (h erein referr ed to as the "City" ), a 
mun ici pal corporation of the State of New Jersey, located in the 
County of Hudson in said State . 
WHEREAS , in 19 49, the City created the Autho rity pur suant 
to Chapter 138 of the la ws of 1946 of New Jersey (t he "S tate" ), 
cons ti tut in g the Se werage Auth orities Law of New Jersey , and 
empowered the Authority to exercise public and governmental 
functions to provide for the public health and we lf are , with all 
ne cessa ry or proper powers to acq uire , constr uct , maintain, 
operate and use the sewerage disposal system (t he "S ewerage 
System" ) referred to the herein ; and 
WHEREAS , to clarif y the res pecti ve respons ibi liti es of the 
City and the Autho rity, the part ies entered into a Sewer Ser vice 
Contr ac t, dated Dece mber 1, 1985 (t he "Se wer Service Cont rac t" ), 
which superceded a previous servi ce contract between the parties 
dated July 1954 as amended on July 1, 1983 ; and 
WHERE AS , under the Sewer Service Contr act , the Cit y has 
agreed to cover any de ficie ncies if, in any year, Authority 
revenues are not suf fic ient to cover the operating and debt 
service requirements of the Autho rity, including bonds is sued by 
the Authority under its general bond resolution (t he "S ewer Bond 
Resolu ti on" ) to construc t, improve or maintain the System; 
provided that the City had consented to the is suance of such 
bonds (r e ferred to hereinafter as ''First Lien Sewer Bonds ") of 
the Autho rity; and 
WHEREAS , in 1998, the Auth ority was reorgan ize d by the City 
as a muni cipal ut iliti es authority pur suant to Chapter 146 of 
the la ws of 1957 of the State , consti tut ing the Muni cipal and 
County Utilities Aut hority Law (t he "Ac t" ) and the Authority 
obtained a leasehold interest in the water system of the City 
(t he "Water Franchi se" ) for a period of 10 yea rs; and WHEREAS , in 1998 , the Authority adopted a new general bond 
resolut ion, dated as of January 28, 1998 and amended on March 
31, 1998 (t he "General Bond Resolution" ) whi ch, among other 
things , precluded the Authority from iss uing additio nal First 
Lien Sewer Bonds under the Sewer Bond Resolution and authori zed 
the is sue of sewer bonds of the Authority pursuant to its terms 
(th e "Sewer Bonds" ); and 
WHEREAS , the Authority and the City are undertaking an 
extension of the Water Franchise and in so doing are ado pt ing an 
amended and restated water franchi se and service agreement ; and 
WHERE AS , the Authority and the City des ire to clarify and 
reaff irm their respecti ve respons ibi lit ies with regard to the 
Sewer System under the Sewer Service Agreement . 
NOW THEREFORE , in consideration of the promises and the 
mutual covenants and agreement set forth and the undertakings of 
each party to the other, the part ies here to , each bin ding 
itse lf, its successors and ass igns , do mutually covenant , 
promi se and agree as follo ws : 
Sect ion 1. Definit ions and Inter pretatio ns . Whenever the 
fol lowing terms , or any of them, are used in the Sewer Ser vice 
Contract or this amendment there to, the same shall be const rued 
and are used and are heretofore and hereafter intended to have 
meanings as follo ws : 
"Bond Resolutio n" shall mean, as applicab le , the 
Sewer Bond Resolution or the General Bond Resolution; 
"Bonds" shall mean (i) the Authorit y' s First Lien 
Bond Sewer Bonds iss ued pursuant to the Sewer 
Resolution or (ii) the Authorit y' s Sewer Bonds iss ued 
pursuant to the General Bond Resolution, or (i ii) 
both, as applicab le; 
''Revenues !! shall mean Sewer Rt:!vtu.u.t:::i:5 under the Gene ral 
Bond Resolut ion; 
"P roj ect" shall mean a Sewer Proj ect as defined in the 
General Bond Resolut ion . 
All other capitalized terms used herein and not 
herein shall have the meanings ass igned to such terms 
General Bond Resolution

(9) If in any calender year , the Sewer Revenues of the 
Authority shall not be su ffic ient to pay or provide 
for (a) Operating Expenses for such cal ender year , (b) 
the sett ing aside of such amounts required by the

General Bond Resolution) or to permit the issu ance of 
Additional Bonds provided that the Proj ect to be 
funded by such Additional Bonds shall have been

and to the extent required under Paragraph 6 of the 
Sewer Service Agreement , the City shall include in its

the Auth ority within 60 days or within 15 days of the

de fic iency in question rela tes , an amount equal to the 
de fi cie ncy .

1 
AMENDED AND RESTATED  
WATER SERVICES FRANCHISE AND SERVICE AGREEMENT 
 
THIS AMENDED AND RESTATED WATER SERVICES FRANCHISE AND 
SERVICE AGREEMENT , made and dated as of the 1st day of February, 2024 (the 
“Agreement”), by and between THE JERSEY CITY MUNICIPAL UTILITIES 
AUTHORITY (the “Authority”), a body politic and corporate, duly organized and existing under 
the Municipal and County Utilities Authorities Law of New Jersey, Chapter 183 of the laws of 
1957 of New Jersey, and the acts amendatory thereof and supplemental thereto (herein referred to 
as the “Act”) and THE CITY OF JERSEY CITY (the “City”), a municipal corporation of the 
State of New Jersey, located in the County of Hudson in said State. The Authority and the City 
shall each be referred to herein individually as a “Party” and collectively as the “Parties”. 
 
W I T N E S S E T H: 
 
WHEREAS, the City currently owns the Water System which provides Water Services, 
as such terms are defined herein, within and outside the geographic boundaries of the City; and 
 
WHEREAS, on February 1, 1998, the Parties entered into that certain Franchise and 
Service Agreement, whereby the City granted the Authority, inter alia, a license to operate the 
Water System (the “Franchise Agreement”); and 
 
 WHEREAS, the Franchise Agreement was amended and restated on May 1, 2003, and 
again on September 30, 2005 (the “ Amended Agreements ” and, together with the Franchise 
Agreement, the “Original Franchise Agreement”); and 
 
WHEREAS, to better enable the Authority to plan for the long-term improvement and 
operation of the Water System and to meet the water needs of the residents and property owners 
of the City and other users of the Water System, the Parties desire to enter into this Agreement; 
and 
 
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the premises and the mutual covenants and 
agreements herein set forth and the undertakings of each Party to the other, the Parties hereto, each 
binding itself, its successors and assigns, do mutually covenant, promise, and agree as follows: 
   
2 
 
ARTICLE I 
 
DEFINITIONS 
 
 Section 101. Definitions.  Whenever the following terms, or any of them, are used in this 
Agreement, the same shall be construed and are used and are intended to have meanings as follows 
except or as otherwise set forth in the Preambles hereof: 
 
“Accountant” shall mean a registered municipal accountant or a certified public accountant 
of the State of New Jersey; 
 
“Act” shall mean the Municipal and County Utilities Authorities Law, N.J.S.A. 40:14B-1, 
et seq.; 
 
“Annual Franchise Fee” shall mean $23,000,000;  
 
“Annual Charge” shall have the meaning given to such term in Section 302 hereof; 
 
“Bond” shall mean any bond, note or other evidence of indebtedness issued, whether 
previously or in the future, by the Authority for the Water System, including any bond, note or 
other evidence of indebtedness issued by the Authority to refund any Bonds. Bonds which are 
deemed to be paid (defeased) in accordance with their terms shall not be considered outstanding 
for the purposes of this Agreement; provided however, Bonds which are paid by proceeds of any 
municipal bond insurance policy shall not be deemed paid and shall remain outstanding for the 
purposes of this Agreement until paid in accordance with any Bond Resolution; 
 
“Bond Resolution” shall mean (i) that certain resolution of the Authority, duly adopted 
January 28, 1998 and entitled “Resolution Authorizing the Issuance of Revenue Bonds of the 
Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority”, as amended and supplemented, (ii) that certain 
resolution of the Authority, duly adopted April 28, 2011 and entitled “Resolution Authorizing the 
Issuance of Water Project Notes of the Jersey City Municipal Utilities Authority”, as amended and 
supplemented, or (iii) any other resolution of the Authority authorizing the issuance of Bonds, 
including without limitation, subordinate Bonds; 
 
“City Water Bonds” shall mean the bonds and bond anticipation notes of the City 
previously issued in connection with the Water System and outstanding on the date of this 
Agreement in the aggregate principal amount of $4,825,000 as shown on Schedule A attached 
hereto, as amended from time to time by written certification of the City; 
 
 “Effective Date” shall mean the date of execution of this Agreement; 
 
“Extended Franchise Period” means a forty (40) year period of time from the Effective 
Date until December 28, 2063; 
  
3 
 “Fiscal Law” shall mean the Local Authorities Fiscal Control Law, N.J.S.A. 40A:5A-1, et 
seq.; 
 
“Fiscal Year” shall mean the period of twelve (12) consecutive months as shall be 
determined from time to time by resolution of the Authority which until further provided shall be 
deemed to be the period commencing on January 1 and ending on December 31; 
 
“New Jersey Local Budget Law” shall mean the New Jersey Local Budget Law, N.J.S.A. 
40A:4-1, et seq.; 
 
“Prior Annual Charge Deficiency” shall have the meaning given to such term in Section 
303 hereof; 
 
“Private Operating Agreement” means the Professional Operations Services Agreement 
between the Authority and Veolia North America, LLC , fka United Water Resources, Inc., in 
connection with the Water System, or any substitute, extension or amendment, or such other 
agreement between the Authority and any public or private entity authorized to operate, maintain, 
manage and/or improve the Water System; 
 
“Sections or Articles” mentioned by numbers are the respective Sections and Articles of 
the Agreement so numbered; 
 
“Service Charges” shall mean all rates, fees, charges and money charged and collected in 
connection with the use and operation of the Water System, including, without limitation, all water 
service charges, rates, rentals, permit fees, application fees, connection fees and other charges; 
 
“Services” shall mean the Water Services otherwise referenced herein; 
 
“Sewer Service Agreement” shall mean the agreement between the City and the Authority 
in connection with the operation by the Authority of the Sewer System, dated December 1, 1985, 
as amended and supplemented. 
 
“Sewer System” shall mean the plans, structures, on-site wastewater systems and any other 
real and personal property acquired, constructed or operated, or to be acquired, constructed, 
maintained or operated by the Authority, including without limitation sewers, conduits, manholes, 
catch basins, tide gates, regulators, pipelines, mains, pumping and ventilation stations, sewage 
treatment or disposal systems, plants, structures, boats, conveyances, licenses, permits, contracts 
and other real and personal property, and rights therein, and appurtenances necessary or useful and 
convenient for the collection, treatment, purification or disposal in a sanitary manner of any 
sanitary, storm or combined sewage, liquid or solid waste, night soil or industrial wastes; 
 
“State” shall mean the State of New Jersey.  
 
“Term of the Water Franchise” shall mean the Extended Franchise Period; 
  
4 
“Unrestricted Fund Balance” means any funds derived from or related to the Water System 
or Sewer System which may be available for use by the Authority for any purpose, and which are 
not required by any contract of the Authority or any Bond Resolution to be retained in any fund or 
account established for the security of the Bonds; 
 
“Water Services” shall mean all those services customarily provided and necessary and 
convenient in connection with the operation of the Water System; 
 
“Water Supply Agreements” shall mean those agreements entered into by the City with 
other parties providing for the supply of water by the City to such parties, more specifically 
identified in Schedule B hereto; 
 
“Water System” shall mean the plans, structures and other personal property acquired, 
constructed or operated by the City and to be operated by the Authority, including reservoirs, 
basins, dams, canals, aqueducts, stand pipes, conduits, pipelines, mains, chlorination facilities, 
pumping stations, water distribution systems, compensating reservoirs, water works or sources of 
water supply, wells, treatment, purification or filtration plans, or other plans and works, 
connections, rights of flowage or diversion, licenses, permits, contracts, structures, boats, 
conveyances, and personal property, and rights therein, and appurtenances necessary or useful and 
convenient for the accumulation, supply or distribution of water, and real property leased or 
otherwise made available by the City to the Authority; 
 
“Year” or “year” means the period of time beginning on July 1 and ending on June 30; 
 
Any terms utilized herein which are not defined and for which the definitions are not clearly 
ascertainable from their context, shall have ascribed to them the meaning set forth in the Act. 
Words importing the singular number include the plural number, and vice versa, and words 
importing persons include firms, associations and corporations. 
 
Section 102. Short Title. This Agreement may hereafter be cited and referred to as the 
“Agreement” or the “Amended and Restated Water Services Franchise and Service Agreement”. 
   
5 
ARTICLE II 
 
EXTENSION OF LICENSE BY CITY OF WATER SYSTEM OPERATING 
FRANCHISE; OPERATION OF WATER SYSTEM; TERM; IMPROVEMENTS 
 
 Section 201.  Extension for Additional Term of License of Water System Operating 
Franchise from City to Authority; Term. 
 
 (a) Pursuant to N.J.S.A. 40:14B-48, the City hereby grants and conveys to the 
Authority for the Term of the Water Franchise all of its rights to operate the Water System, 
including all permits, approvals, licenses, contracts, revenues and all other related rights associated 
therewith. Such conveyance shall constitute a grant and license to and in the Water System to the 
Authority, subject only to the provisions of this Agreement. 
 
 (b) The Authority hereby accepts such grant and license, including all obligations of 
the City in connection therewith, and including all Water Supply Agreements. 
 
 (c) To the extent that the City is unable to assign its rights and/or obligations under the 
provisions of any agreements it has in connection with the operations or the supply of water in 
connection with the Water System, all as referenced herein, then the license of the franchise 
provided for herein shall be deemed to be subject to such agreements not otherwise assigned or 
amended. Such event will not affect the obligations otherwise set forth herein of either Party. The 
City hereby agrees to continue to provide the services called for in such agreements, either directly 
or through the Authority, until such assignment or amendment occurs. 
 
 Section 202. Consideration for License of Rights to Water System.  As consideration for 
the right to operate the Water System and to receive the revenues associated therewith, the 
Authority agrees that for the Term of the Water Franchise it shall: 
 
(a) Continue to assume the responsibility for and the payment of the principal and 
interest on the City Water Bonds and any other such amounts otherwise agreed to herein for the 
Term of the Water Franchise; 
 
(b) Pay, on the Effective Date, and on each anniversary thereof, the Annual Franchise 
Fee. 
 
(c) In addition to the Annual Franchise Fee,  
 
 (i) Pay, within ninety (90) days from the Effective Date, subject to and 
conditioned upon the approval of the Local Finance Board and the successful sale of necessary 
Bonds, the amount of $30,000,000, which amount shall be fully recoverable by the Authority via 
the Annual Charges; and 
 
 (ii) Pay in 2025, within thirty (30) days of the successful sale of necessary 
Bonds, the amount of $10,000,000, which amount shall be fully recoverable by the Authority via 
the Annual Charges; and  
6 
 
 (iii) Pay in 2026, within thirty (30) days of the successful sale of necessary 
Bonds, the amount of $10,000,000, which amount shall be fully recoverable by the Authority via 
the Annual Charges; and 
 
 (d) Provide Water Services and Sewer System services to the City free of charge for 
all governmental public facilities of the City as set forth on a list supplied to the Authority by the 
City from time to time during the Term of the Water Franchise. 
  
 Section 203. Construction, Installation, Maintenance and Operation by Authority 
of Extensions or Improvements of Water System. 
 
 (a) The Parties hereby agree that Authority shall undertake, construct and install any 
and all extensions or improvements to the Water System as it deems necessary or desirable, in its 
sole discretion. The Parties hereby acknowledge, understand and agree that the Authority may, in 
its sole discretion, issue Bonds for such purposes.  
 
(b) The Authority agrees to operate and maintain the Water System on behalf of the 
City at all times during the Term of the Water Franchise in compliance with all federal, State and 
local laws and regulations and in accordance with the terms and provisions of this Agreement. The 
Authority further agrees that the Water System shall at all times be kept in good repair and working 
order and that such Water System shall be managed, operated and maintained by the Authority in 
an efficient and economical manner, in accordance with the terms and provisions of this 
Agreement and otherwise in accordance with prudent utility practice.  
 
(c) The City shall have the right at any time to inspect the Water System and/or the 
operation thereof by the Authority. 
 
(d) The Authority shall keep such records of all pertinent operating data and 
information relating to the Water System, including accounting and financial records, as prudent 
utility practice shall require. The City shall have continuous and unrestricted access to such 
records. 
 
Section 204. Water Utility Liens; Authority Liens. During the Term of the Water 
Franchise, the Authority and the City agree that the enforcement of the Authority's Service Charges 
for Water Services will be governed by and provided for in accordance with the provisions of 
N.J.S.A. 40:14B-45. 
 
Section 205. Financing Improvements by City Bonds. The City hereby agrees that if it 
finances any additions, modifications or improvements to the Water System by the issuance of its 
own City bonds, notes or other obligations, it will not pledge any of the water revenues generated 
from the Water System to the holders of any such City bonds, notes or other obligations, other 
than those City Water Bonds previously issued and referred to in Schedule A attached hereto.  
 
Section 206. Authority Rules and Regulations.  The Authority shall maintain and 
update, as necessary, rules and regulations for the users of the Water System including technical  
7 
specifications, a statement of the Authority’s fees and other charges, such as all miscellaneous fees 
and charges for new water meters, water taps, late payment fees, service shut-offs, other similar 
services, bulk water rates, and the procedures in connection with the issuance of water 
emergencies, as well as all other provisions necessary to operate, maintain, and manage the Water 
System as provided herein.  
   
8 
ARTICLE III 
 
CHARGES BY THE AUTHORITY 
 
 Section 301. Service Charges and Annual Charge Payments. 
 
 (a) With respect to all water supplied by or on behalf of the Authority and for all use 
and services of the Water System during the Term of the Water Franchise (with the exception of 
the Water Services to the City's public governmental facilities referred to in Section 202(d) hereof), 
the Authority will charge Service Charges in accordance with the Act. Said Service Charges shall 
be established at rates estimated by the Authority to be sufficient to provide for the receipt by the 
Authority in each Fiscal Year of a sum of money at least equal to the sum of all the amounts 
necessary in such Fiscal Year, to the extent not provided from other funds: 
 
 (i) to pay or provide for the expenses of operation and maintenance of the 
Water System, including any amount required to be paid pursuant to this Agreement, and the 
principal of and interest on any and all Bonds of the Authority and the City Water Bonds, as the 
same shall become due and payable; 
 
 (ii) to maintain such reserves or sinking funds as may be required by the terms 
of any contract of the Authority or any Bond Resolution, or as may be deemed necessary or 
desirable by the Authority; and 
 
 (iii) to comply in all respects with the terms and provisions of any Bond 
Resolution and the Act, provided however, nothing contained in this Section 301 or this Agreement 
shall prevent the Authority from charging Service Charges which shall be less than sufficient to 
comply with the foregoing requirement, provided that the City shall have given their written 
consent to such lesser Service Charges in the manner herein provided. 
 
 (b) The Authority reserves the right to issue any Bond in a manner that involves the 
Water System such that the Service Charges therefor are calculated based on the structure of the 
security for the Bonds. The calculation of the Annual Charge shall similarly be based on the Bond 
structure utilized. 
 
 (c) The Authority, either directly or through its agents to the extent appropriate, shall 
(i) direct sufficient funds to pay or provide for the principal of and interest on any of the Bonds 
issued by the Authority therefor as the same become due and payable, (ii) to maintain such reserves 
or sinking funds as may be required by the terms of any Bond Resolution and (iii) to comply in all 
respects with the terms and provisions of any Bond Resolution and the Act. 
 
 Section 302. Annual Charges. In any year in which the Authority shall have a deficiency 
whereby the Authority shall not have sufficient funds to provide for the items listed in Section 301 
hereof, the City shall pay an Annual Charge to the Authority. The Annual Charge due and payable 
in any Fiscal Year shall be the sum of money equal to the excess, if any, of: 
  
9 
(a) The sum of all of the amounts necessary or expended in the Fiscal Year of the 
Authority during such year (i) to pay or provide for the expenses of operation and maintenance of 
the Water System during the Term of the Water Franchise, including, without limitation, 
administrative and other expenses of the Authority, the Annual Franchise Fee, insurance, renewals, 
replacements, extensions, enlargements, alterations and betterment of the Water System not 
otherwise provided by the City as set forth herein and the principal or redemption price of and 
interest on all Bonds of the Authority issued for the Water System and of the City Water Bonds as 
the same become due and payable; (ii) to maintain such reserves or sinking funds as may be 
required by the terms of any contract of the Authority or any Bond Resolution, or as may otherwise 
be deemed necessary by the Authority; and (iii) to comply in all respects with the terms of any 
Bond Resolution and with the Act; from which shall be subtracted 
 
(b) The sum of the following amounts to the extent available to be used to pay or 
provide for the amounts described in sub-section (a) above: (i) Service Charges collected by or on 
behalf of the Authority; (ii) the proceeds of insurance awards received by or for account of the 
Authority with respect to the Water System during the Term of the Water Franchise; (iii) 
contributions received by or for account of the Authority with respect to the Water System and not 
under any circumstances repayable by the Authority until after the payment in full of all other 
obligations of the Authority including its Bonds; or (iv) any other funds of the Authority on hand 
and available therefor at the beginning of the Fiscal Year. 
 
Section 303. Payment of Annual Charge and Prior Annual Charge Deficiency by the 
City. 
 
 (a) In the event the Authority shall have a deficiency and the Annual Charge, as 
calculated in Section 302, is payable by the City, then on or before February 1, the Authority will 
make and deliver to the City its statement stating the amount of the Annual Charge due from the 
City for the preceding Fiscal Year computed in accordance with this Agreement (herein called the 
“Prior Annual Charge Deficiency ”). The Prior Annual Charge Deficiency for such preceding 
Fiscal Year, to the extent that any part thereof shall not have been theretofore paid, shall in all 
events be due and payable from the City no later than August 15 next succeeding the close of such 
Fiscal Year. 
 
 (b) On or before February 1 of each year after the Effective Date, the Authority shall 
certify to the City in writing the amount of the Annual Charge which will become payable by the 
City for the coming Fiscal Year. On or before August 15 of each Fiscal Year, the City will pay to 
the Authority the amount of the Annual Charge set forth in the certificate delivered to it by the 
Authority. 
 
 (c) The City will, in each year, make all budgetary and other provisions or 
appropriations necessary to provide for and authorize the payment by the City to the Authority 
during each year of the estimated amount of the Prior Annual Charge Deficiency and/or the amount 
of the Annual Charge out of the first funds becoming legally available to the City for this purpose 
and to provide the funds for such payments to the Authority, if not otherwise available, from the 
levy of ad valorem taxes upon all the taxable real property located in the City without limitation 
as to rate or amount.  
10 
 
 (d) In the event that the amount of the Annual Charge paid by the City for any year 
shall be more than the actual amount of such Annual Charge, the Authority will credit the 
difference between said amounts to the City against the amount of the next payment becoming due 
to the Authority from the City under this Section or reimburse the City to the extent such excess 
is not required under any resolution of the Authority. 
 
Section 304. Collection of Service Charges.  The Authority will at all times take all 
reasonable measures permitted by the Act or otherwise by law to collect and enforce prompt 
payment to or for all Service Charges. 
   
11 
ARTICLE IV 
 
AUTHORITY ACTIONS 
 
 Section 401. Authority Budgets, Service Charges, Minutes and Books and Records.  
 
 (a) The Authority shall approve and adopt annual budgets prescribed by any applicable 
Bond Resolution and by applicable law, including without limitation, the Fiscal Law, including 
any increases or decreases in Service Charges, to reflect the revenues and expenses in such 
budgets. As consideration for the obligations of the City to make payment of the Annual Charges 
hereunder, and to the extent permitted by applicable law, the Authority hereby agrees that a copy 
of any proposed annual budget or amended annual budget and any increases or decreases in Service 
Charges to be adopted shall be filed in the office of the City Clerk of the City not less than thirty 
(30) days prior to the adoption of such budget. 
 
 (b) The Authority hereby agrees that it will establish Service Charges annually at levels 
to assure that the City will annually be able to treat any outstanding City Water Bonds and any 
other City Water Bonds that it issues in the future as self-liquidating for purposes of the New 
Jersey Local Budget Law. To the extent, however, that the City’s actions with regard to matters 
included in the budget or otherwise prevent the Authority from enacting increases in Service 
Charges in connection with the Water System as required to comply with the Authority's Bond 
Resolution, the City agrees that the Annual Charge will be increased to insure that the amount of 
revenue and other funds received by the Authority are sufficient to operate and maintain the Water 
System as well as pay debt service on the Bonds issued therefor and consistent with the Authority's 
obligations under any Bond Resolution and other documents. 
 
 (c)  To the extent permitted by law, the Authority hereby agrees that all minutes of the 
Authority and any resolutions, contracts, agreements or similar documents approved by the 
Authority, shall be available to the City for inspection and copying, upon reasonable notice, at any 
time during normal business hours, at the offices of the Authority 
 
 (d) The Authority will keep proper books of record and account in which complete and 
correct entries shall be made of its transactions relating to the Water System or any part thereof, 
and which, together with all other books and papers of the Authority, shall at all reasonable times 
be subject to inspection by the City. The Authority shall cause its books and accounts to be audited 
annually by an Accountant, and annually within six (6) months after the close of each Fiscal Year, 
copies of the report of such audits so made shall be furnished by the Authority to the City, including 
statements in reasonable detail, accompanied by a certificate signed by the Accountant, of financial 
condition, of revenues and operating expenses, and of all funds held by or for the Authority within 
seven (7) days of its completion. 
   
12 
ARTICLE V 
 
MISCELLANEOUS 
 
 Section 501. Enforcement and Obligation of Annual Charge. If any payment or part 
thereof due to the Authority from the City shall remain unpaid for thirty (30) days following its 
due date, the City shall be charged with and will pay to the Authority interest on the amount unpaid 
from its due date until paid, at a rate per annum equal to one percent (1%) in excess of the highest 
rate of interest per annum which the Authority is then paying with respect to any of its Bonds, 
unless waived by the Authority. Every obligation assumed by or imposed upon the City by this 
Agreement shall be enforceable by the Authority by appropriate action or proceeding, and the 
Authority may have and pursue any and all remedies provided by law for the enforcement of such 
obligation including the remedies and processes provided by the Act with respect to charges. 
 
Section 502. Effect of Breach.  Failure on the part of the Authority or the City in any 
instance or under any circumstance to observe or fully perform any obligation assumed by or 
imposed upon it by this Agreement or by law shall not relieve the City from making any payment 
to the Authority or fully performing any other obligation required of it under this Agreement, but 
the City may have and pursue any and all other remedies provided by law for compelling 
performance by the Authority of said obligation assumed by or imposed upon the Authority. 
 
 Section 503. Pledge or Assignment; Amendment of Certain Provisions.  This 
Agreement may not be assigned by any Party without the consent of the other Party; provided, 
however, that, since this Agreement is made with particular reference to the holders or prospective 
holders of Bonds or other obligations of the Authority for the purpose of assuring and protecting 
the interests of such holders, the Authority, without consent of the City, at any time may assign or 
pledge for the benefit and security of the holders of Bonds all of its rights under the provisions of 
this Agreement to receive payments from the City.  
 
 Section 5.04. Ratification of Prior Bonds.  The City and the Authority hereby ratify the 
issuance of all Bonds of the Authority issued prior to the Effective Date and hereby agree and 
confirm that such prior Bonds remain benefitted and secured by this Agreement. 
 
Section 505. Indemnification of City by Authority.  The Authority hereby assumes 
responsibility for the City's obligation and liabilities in connection with the operation of the Water 
System during the Term of the Water Franchise. 
 
Section 506. Repayments to City.  It is the intention of the City and the Authority that 
obligations and expenses of the Authority be paid from revenues of the Water System. The 
Authority and the City further intend that the City be reimbursed for any amounts paid under this 
Agreement from the Unrestricted Fund Balance of the Authority at such time as the Authority's 
operations will permit. 
 
 Section 507. Modification or Amendment of this Agreement.  This Agreement may be 
modified or amended by the written consent of the Parties, provided however, that in the event any 
Bonds remain outstanding in accordance with the terms of any Bond Resolution, no such  
13 
modification or amendment shall (i) change or alter the obligation and requirement of the City to 
pay the Annual Charge to the Authority in accordance with the terms herein, (ii) change or alter 
the obligation and requirement of the Authority to operate the Water System and establish, charge 
and collect Service Charges in accordance with terms herein, or (iii) make any other change in this 
Agreement that shall have a material adverse effect on the Authority’s ability to pay the principal 
of, redemption premium, if any, and interest on the Bonds. 
 
 Section 508. Termination of this Agreement. At any time after the payment in full of 
all obligations of the Authority, including its Bonds and any Bonds issued to refund any Bonds, 
the City or the Authority, may terminate this Agreement.  
 
Section 509. Execution in Counterparts. This Agreement may be executed in any 
number of counterparts, each of which shall be executed by the Authority and by the City and all 
of which shall be regarded for all purposes as one original and shall constitute and be but one and 
the same. 
 
Section 510. Severability of Provisions.  If any one or more of the covenants or 
agreements provided in this Agreement on the part of the Authority or the City to be performed, 
should be contrary to law, then such covenant or covenants, agreement or agreements, shall be 
deemed separable from the remaining covenants and agreements, and shall in no way affect the 
validity of the other provisions of this Agreement. 
 
Section 511. Agreement to be in Addition to Sewer Service Agreement. The Sewer 
Service Agreement shall continue to be effective to govern the rights and obligations of the City 
and the Authority in connection with the operation of the Sewer System and shall not be considered 
in any way as affected by the provisions of this Agreement. 
 
 Section 512. Original Franchise Agreement.  Upon the Effective Date, the Original 
Franchise Agreement shall automatically terminate in accordance with its terms without any 
further action of the Parties. 
 
 Section 513. Effective Date of Agreement. This Agreement shall take effect upon the 
Effective Date.
